### p1208[0...4] BI: Automatic restart control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0   [ 1 ] 0   [ 2 ] 0   [ 3 ] 1   [ 4 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the control signals of the automatic restart.  
For index [0]:  
Sets the signal source for the signal "line supply OK".  
After a power failure/fault, the automatic restart function evaluates the signal "line
supply OK" in order to switch on again.  
Recommended interconnection:  
p1208[0] = r6530.0 (signal source to own drive object or to A_INFMV)  
For index [1] (only SINAMICS SM - VECTORMV):  
Sets the signal source for the signal "drive stop".  
Recommended interconnection:  
p1208[1] = r1214.9 (signal source to A_INFMV)  
For index [2] (only SINAMICS SM - VECTORMV):  
Sets the signal source for the signal "drive restart".  
Using this signal, the status signal r1214.6 is also influenced (delay time runs automatically
after powering up).  
Recommended interconnection:  
p1208[2] = r1214.6 (signal source to A_INFMV)  
For index [3] (only SINAMICS SL):  
Sets the signal source for the external enable of the automatic restart (AR).  
This signal is used to enable the function set in p1210.  
For index [4]:  
Sets the signal source for the signal "VSM available".  
When switching on again, the "Line OK" signal is evaluated using this signal.  
Recommended interconnection:  
p1208[4] = r6530.1

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Infeed line supply voltage OK  
[
1]:
Drive stop  
[
2]:
Drive restart  
[
3]:
AR enable external  
[
4]:
Voltage Sensing Module available

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1214, r6530

### p1209 Automatic restart alarm time before restarting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6762, 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 30.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm time before the automatic restart.  
The alarm time is started after the automatic restart successfully acknowledged all
faults, internally generated an ON command and has received the operating signal from
the infeed.  
Once the alarm time has expired, the pulses for the vector drive are enabled and the
speed setpoint becomes effective.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1214

**Caution:**
  
The alarm time is not effective for motor speeds higher than 100 rpm.

**Note:**
  
This alarm time is displayed via status signal r1214.6 = 1.

### p1210 Automatic restart mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6762, 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 6 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the automatic restart mode (AR).

**Recommend.:**
  
For brief line supply failures, the motor shaft may still be rotating when restarting.
The "flying restart" function (p1200) might need to be activated to restart while
the motor shaft is still rotating.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inhibit automatic restart  
4:
Automatic restart only after line supply failure  
6:
Restart after every fault

**Dependency:**
  
The automatic restart requires an active ON command (e.g., via a digital input). If,
for p1210 > 1, there is no active ON command, then the automatic restart is interrupted.
With the ON command, STW1.10 must be 1 (master control by PLC).  
When using an Advanced Operator Panel (AOP) in the LOCAL Mode, then there is no automatic
restart.  
  
Refer to:
p1212, p1213, r1214  
Refer to:
F07320

**Danger:**
  
If the automatic restart is activated (p1210 > 1) if there is an ON command (refer
to p0840), the drive is switched on as soon as any fault messages that are present
can be acknowledged. This also occurs after the line supply returns or the Control
Unit boots if the DC link voltage is again present or the feedback of the line supply
infeed (refer to p0864) is again available. This automatic switching-on operation
can only be interrupted by withdrawing the ON command.

**Notice:**
  
For the motor (p6650 > 0), when the test mode is activated, AR is automatically deactivated.  
For active AR (r1214), all of the faults of the infeed are acknowledged - even if
the infeed is in the test mode.  
Changing the response to fault F07405 can mean that after a brief line dip, the motor
starts again from standstill.

**Note:**
  
For brief line supply failures, the motor shaft may still be rotating when restarting.  
In order to restart while the motor shaft is still rotating, the "flying restart"
function should be internally activated.  
For p1210 = 4 (automatic restart after F05206 and extended buffering using precharging):  
Line faults, caused by a short line interruption, are suppressed. During the line
interruption, the drive is in the "Ready" state, and the circuit breaker remains closed.
The DC link is supported by precharging. Operation is resumed again as soon as the
line supply is fault-free.  
An automatic restart is only executed if fault F05206 has occurred.  
If additional faults are present, then these faults are also acknowledged and when
successful, starting continues.  
If an ON command is active, it is only possible to change the automatic restart mode
from 4 to 6 if, at the same time, the operating signal is present.  
For p1210 = 6 (automatic restart after each fault and extended buffering using precharging):  
Same response as for p1210 = 4. An automatic restart is carried out after each Motor
Module fault.  
For p1210 = 4, 6:  
If the extended buffering (p1210 = 4, 6) and the kinetic buffering (p1240) are simultaneously
used during a brief line interruption, the kinetic buffering (Vdc_min controller)
is deactivated when the minimum speed (p1257) is reached and fault F07405 is output.
The extended buffering is always canceled and an automatic restart does not occur.  
When required, for fault F07405, as response NONE can be set (p2100 and following).

### p1212 Automatic restart delay time start attempts

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6762, 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [s] | 1000.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for the start attempt.  
If the automatic restart (AR) is active, initially the system waits for the line supply
to return within the time set in p1213 (for p1213 = 0, the system waits for an unlimited
time for the line supply to return). For binector input p1208 = 0/1 signal (line supply
OK), after the wait time expires, all of the faults that are present are automatically
acknowledged and the ON command is internally generated.  
In the case of extended buffering, the pulses are enabled after the line returns and
after the wait time has expired.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1213

**Notice:**
  
A change is only accepted and made in the state "initialization" (r1214.0) and "wait
for alarm" (r1214.1).

### p1213 Automatic restart monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 1999.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the automatic restart (AR).  
The monitoring time is started after power failure or a fault. The drive must be operational
again within this time otherwise an appropriate fault is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1210, p1212, r1214  
Refer to:
F07320

**Note:**
  
For p1213 = 0, the following applies: The monitoring is deactivated.  
When the monitoring is activated, the following applies: p1213 > p1212

### r1214.0...12 CO/BO: Automatic restart status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6762, 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the automatic restart (AR).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Initialization | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Enable AR and wait for alarm | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Auto restart act | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Acknowledge alarms | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Automatic restart command | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Delay time running after automatic switch-on | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Fault | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Extended buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Activating two-level control | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Pulse inhibit acknowledge internal | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Starting attempt monitoring time running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Fault suppression active | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
State to display the single initialization after POWER ON.  
For bit 01:  
State in which the automatic restart function waits for faults (initial state).  
For bit 02:  
General display that a fault has been identified and that the restart or acknowledgment
has been initiated.  
For bit 04:  
State in which the faults that are present are acknowledged. The state is exited again
after successful acknowledgment.  
For example, this signal can be used to acknowledge on another drive object (BI: p2103).  
For bit 05:  
State in which the drive is automatically switched on.  
For bit 06:  
State in which the system waits after having been switched on, to the end of the start
attempt. The delay time in p1209 runs.  
For example, this signal can be used via an output terminal to provide an acoustic
display.  
For bit 07:  
State which is assumed after a fault occurs within the automatic restart function.  
For bit 08:  
Extended buffering using precharging or buffering brief power failures is active.
After a power failure, the drive is switched on again.  
For bit 09:  
State in which the extended buffering function switches-in precharging. The drive
waits in this state for the line supply to return.  
For bit 10:  
State in which the PSA internally acknowledges the pulse inhibit.  
For bit 11:  
After successfully switching on again, the monitoring time runs (10 s). If a fault
occurs during this time, then the restart is not started again.  
For bit 12:  
State, in which fault suppression is requested on the line side.

### p1240[0...n] Vdc controller or Vdc monitoring configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 6 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the controller configuration of the DC link voltage (Vdc controller) in the closed-loop
control mode.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inhibit Vdc controller  
1:
Enable Vdc_max controller  
2:
Enable Vdc_min controller (kinetic buffering)  
3:
Enable Vdc_min controller and Vdc_max controller  
4:
Activate Vdc_max monitoring  
5:
Activate Vdc_min monitoring  
6:
Activate Vdc_min monitoring and Vdc_max monitoring

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1245  
Refer to:
A07400, A07401, A07402, F07403, F07404, F07405, F07406

**Caution:**
  
If, for kinetic buffering, after the minimum speed has been reached, the automatic
restart (AR) is to be activated, the responses of faults F07405 and F07406 must be
set to "none".  
When using kinetic buffering in conjunction with a Voltage Sensing Module (VSM), the
automatic restart should be activated in order to suppress the response to line supply
faults therefore preventing immediate drive shutdown.

**Notice:**
  
An excessively high value in p1245 can possibly negatively influence the normal operation
of the drive.

**Note:**
  
p1240 = 1, 3:  
When the DC link voltage limit specified for the Motor Module is reached the following
applies:  
- the Vdc_max controller limits the regenerative energy in order that the DC link
voltage is kept below the maximum DC link voltage when braking.  
- the ramp-down times are automatically increased.  
p1240 = 2, 3:  
When the switch-in threshold of the Vdc_min controller is reached (p1245), the following
applies:  
- the Vdc_min controller limits the energy taken from the DC link in order to keep
the DC link voltage above the minimum DC link voltage when accelerating.  
- the motor is braked in order to use its kinetic energy to buffer the DC link.  
p1240 = 4, 5, 6:  
When the threshold in r1242 or r1246 is reached, the DC link voltage monitoring initiates
a fault (F07403 or F07404) with a response and therefore reduces additional negative
effects on the DC link voltage.

### p1241[0...n] Vdc_max controller switch-in level

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 95 [%] | 140 [%] | [ 0 ] 106 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the switch-in level for the Vdc_max controller.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1242, p3510

**Notice:**
  
For an M2C converter, p1241 does not refer to p0210, but to p3510.  
The absolute switch-on level is therefore p1241[%] * p3510[V].

### r1242 Vdc_max controller switch-in level

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
For an M2C converter, r1242 is not valid.  
Here, the activation level of the Vdc_max controller is calculated from p3510 * p1241.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1241, p3510

**Note:**
  
The Vdc_max controller is only deactivated again if the module voltage r0070 falls
below threshold 0.95 * p3510 * p1241 - and the controller output is zero.

### p1243[0...n] Vdc_max controller dynamic factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 [%] | 10000 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the dynamic factor for the DC link voltage controller (Vdc_max controller).  
100% means that p1250, p1251, and p1252 (gain, integral time, and rate time) are used
corresponding to their basic settings and based on a theoretical controller optimization.  
If subsequent optimization is required, this can be carried out using the dynamic
factor. In this case p1250, p1251, p1252 are weighted with the dynamic factor p1243.  
If several modules are connected to the DC link, then the dynamic factor must be increased
corresponding to the ratio of the additional capacitances to the capacitance of the
module involved.

**Note:**
  
The pre-setting of the dynamic factor is based on the power units connected to DRIVE-CLiQ.
It is assumed that the power units connected via DRIVE-CLiQ are also electrically
connected to the DC link. If this is not the case, then the dynamic factor must be
optimized manually.

### p1245[0...n] Vdc_min controller switch-in level (kinetic buffering)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50 [%] | 100 [%] | [ 0 ] 90 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the switch-in level for the Vdc-min controller (kinetic buffering).  
The value is obtained as follows:  
AC/AC device: r1246[V] = p1245[%] * sqrt(2) * p0210  
DC/AC device: r1246[V] = p1245[%] * p0210

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0210

**Warning:**
  
An excessively high value possibly negatively influences normal drive operation, and
can mean that after the line supply returns, the Vdc minimum control can no longer
be exited.  
The values up to 150 % are intended for operating modes p1240 = 5, 6.

**Note:**
  
For SINAMICS GM/SM, the following applies:  
Minimum value = 0.75  
Maximum value = 0.90

### r1246 Vdc_min controller switch-in level (kinetic buffering)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
For an M2C converter, r1246 is not valid.  
Here, the activation level of the Vdc_min controller is calculated from p3510 * p1245.

**Note:**
  
The Vdc_min controller is only deactivated again if the module voltage r0070 exceeds
threshold 1.05 * p3510 * p1241 - and the controller output is zero.

### p1247[0...n] Vdc_min controller dynamic factor (kinetic buffering)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the dynamic factor for the Vdc_min controller (kinetic buffering).  
100% means that p1250, p1251, and p1252 (gain, integral time, and rate time) are used
corresponding to their basic settings and based on a theoretical controller optimization.  
If subsequent optimization is required, this can be carried out using the dynamic
factor. In this case p1250, p1251, p1252 are weighted with the dynamic factor p1247.  
If several components are connected to the DC link, then the dynamic factor must be
increased corresponding to the ratio of the additional capacitances to the capacitance
of the component involved.

### p1249[0...n] Vdc_max controller speed threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [rpm] | 30000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower speed threshold for the Vdc_max controller.  
When this speed threshold is undershot, the Vdc_max control is switched out and the
speed is controlled using the ramp-function generator.

**Note:**
  
For fast braking where the ramp-function generator tracking was active, it is possible
to prevent the drive rotating in the opposite direction by increasing the speed threshold
and setting a final rounding-off time in the ramp-function generator (p1131). This
is supported using a dynamic setting of the speed controller.

### p1250[0...n] Vdc controller proportional gain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 | 100.00 | [ 0 ] 1.00 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the proportional gain for the DC link voltage controller (Vdc_min controller,
Vdc_max controller).

**Dependency:**
  
The effective proportional gain is obtained taking into account p1243 (Vdc_max controller
dynamic factor).

**Note:**
  
The gain factor is proportional to the capacitance of the DC link. The parameter is
pre-set to a value that is optimally adapted to the capacitance of the individual
Motor Module. The capacitances of the other power units, which are connected to the
DC link, can be taken into account using the dynamic factor (p1247 or p1243).

### p1251[0...n] Vdc controller integral time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 10000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the integral time for the DC link voltage controller (Vdc_min controller, Vdc_max
controller).

**Dependency:**
  
The effective integral time is obtained taking into account p1243 (Vdc_max controller
dynamic factor).

**Note:**
  
An integral time is normally not required for single axis drives. For multi-axis drives
on the other hand, it may be possible to compensate for interference from other axes
using the integral time (integral component) .  
p1251 = 0: The integral component is deactivated.

### p1252[0...n] Vdc controller rate time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 1000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the rate time constant for the DC link voltage controller (Vdc_min controller,
Vdc_max controller).

**Dependency:**
  
The effective rate time is obtained taking into account p1243 (Vdc_max controller
dynamic factor).

### p1255[0...n] Vdc_min controller time threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 10000.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the time threshold for the Vdc_min controller (kinetic buffering).  
If this value is exceeded a fault is output; the required response can be parameterized.  
Prerequisite: p1256 = 1

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F07406

**Notice:**
  
If a time threshold has been parameterized, the Vdc_max controller should also be
activated (p1240 = 3) so that the drive does not shut down with overvoltage when Vdc_min
control is exited (due to the time violation) and in the event of fault response OFF3.
It is also possible to increase the OFF3 ramp-down time p1135.

### p1256[0...n] Vdc_min controller response (kinetic buffering)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the response for the Vdc_min controller (kinetic buffering).

**Value:**
  
0:
Buffer Vdc until undervoltage, n<p1257 -> F07405  
1:
Buff. Vdc until undervolt., n<p1257 -> F07405, t>p1255 -> F07406

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F07405, F07406

**Caution:**
  
If, for kinetic buffering, after the minimum speed has been reached, the automatic
restart (AR) is to be activated, the responses of faults F07405 and F07406 must be
set to "none".  
When using kinetic buffering in conjunction with a Voltage Sensing Module (VSM), the
automatic restart should be activated in order to suppress the response to line supply
faults therefore preventing immediate drive shutdown.

### p1257[0...n] Vdc_min controller speed threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [rpm] | 30000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 50.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the speed threshold for the Vdc-min controller (kinetic buffering).  
If this value is exceeded a fault is output; the required response can be parameterized
.  
Kinetic buffering is not started below the speed threshold.

### r1258 CO: Vdc controller output

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6220 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**6_2 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2002 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Arms] | - [Arms] | [ ] - [Arms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the actual output of the Vdc controller (DC link voltage controller)

**Note:**
  
The regenerative power limit p1531 is used for vector control to precontrol the Vdc_max
controller. The lower the power limit is set, the lower the correction signals of
the controller when the voltage limit is reached.

### p1260 Line transfer configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the line transfer function.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Enable: transfer to the line supply | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Enable: transfer from line supply | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Missing operation able: motor side switch open | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3800

**Note:**
  
The line transfer is always realized synchronized.  
Selecting a synchronoscope in p3800 is absolutely necessary.

### r1261.0...24 CO/BO: Line transfer control word/status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the line transfer control and status word.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Enable transfer to the line supply | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Enable transfer from the line supply | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Line transfer test mode | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Transfer to line is permitted | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Transfer to line requested | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Transfer to line is active | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Transfer to line is completed | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Transfer from line is permitted | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Normal operation or transfer from line supply requested | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Transfer from line is active | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Transfer from line is completed | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Normal operation is permitted | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Normal operation is active | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Line transfer: command close line side switch | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Line transfer: line side switch is closed | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Line transfer: simulation line side switch | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Line transfer: command close motor side switch | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Line transfer: motor side switch is closed | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | Line transfer: simulation motor side switch | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Line transfer: operating enable | No | Yes | - |

### p1262[0...n] Line transfer maximum overlap time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum overlap time in which the output side as well as the motor-line circuit
breakers are closed.  
After the time expires, when transferring to the line supply, the output-side circuit
breaker is opened - or when transferring from the line supply, the motor-line circuit
breaker is opened.  
Message A49947 is also initiated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49947

**Note:**
  
In the "0" setting, overlap is permissible for an unlimited time.

### p1263[0...n] Line transfer frequency gradient during overlap

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 | 1.00 | [ 0 ] 0.50 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the frequency gradient in [Hz/s] while both circuit breakers - the output side
as well as the motor-line circuit breaker - are closed (overlap).  
When transferring to the line supply, the output voltage frequency is decreased with
these gradients.  
When transferring from the line supply, the output voltage frequency is increased
with these gradients.

### p1264[0...n] Line transfer voltage gradient during overlap

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3000 | [ 0 ] 1000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the voltage gradient in [V/s] while both circuit breakers - the output side as
well as the motor-line circuit breaker - are closed (overlap).  
When transferring to the line supply, the output voltage is decreased with these gradients.  
When transferring from the line supply, the output voltage is increased with these
gradients.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** - |

4.8

### p1267[0...n] BI: Line transfer start/request transfer from line supply sig. src.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |

4.8

Sets the signal source for control command "Start normal operation" or "Transfer from
line".  
The signal is indicated in parameter r1261.9.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1260

The start of normal operation or transfer to the line supply is executed if this requirement
is active, and the associated permission is available in parameter r1261.8 or r1261.12.  
Depending on the operating state, the permission is either in r1261.8 or r1261.12.

### p1300[0...n] Open-loop/closed-loop control operating mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6020, 6301, 7605 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 23 | [ 0 ] 20 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the open and closed-loop control mode of a drive.

**Value:**
  
0:
U/f control with linear characteristic  
18:
I/f control with fixed current  
20:
Speed control (encoderless)  
21:
Speed control (with encoder)  
22:
Torque control (encoderless)  
23:
Torque control (with encoder)

**Dependency:**
  
Closed-loop speed or torque control (with encoder) cannot be selected if the encoder
type is not entered (p0400).  
Bypass operation and synchronizing to the line supply (p3800 = 1, 3) is only possible
in the operating mode p1300 = 20.  
Setting p1300 = 0 may only be used when the terminals are open and only for test purposes.
The U/f control is enabled using p1317.  
  
Refer to:
r0108, p0300, p0311, p0400, p1501, p6890

**Note:**
  
The closed-loop torque control can only be changed over in operation (p1300 = 20,
21) by selecting the closed-loop speed control (p1501). At the changeover, the setting
of p1300 does not change. In this case, the actual state is displayed in r1407, bit
2 and bit 3.

### p1317[0...n] U/f control activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 5019 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the U/f control with linear characteristic. The system operates
with up to 20 Hz in closed-loop controlled operation. The characteristic only becomes
active from 20 Hz and above.

**Value:**
  
0:
Deactivated (p1300 effective)  
1:
Activated

### p1338 U/f mode resonance damping gain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 | 100.00 | [ 0 ] 0.00 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the gain for resonance damping for U/f control.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1339, p7595

**Note:**
  
The resonance damping function dampens active current oscillations that frequently
occur under no-load conditions.

### p1340 I_max frequency controller proportional gain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6302 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 | 0.500 | [ 0 ] 0.000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the proportional gain of the I_max voltage controller.  
The I_max controller reduces the drive converter output current if the maximum current
(r0067) is exceeded.  
In the U/f operating modes (p1300) for the I_max control, one controller is used that
acts on the output frequency. The frequency controller reduces the current by decreasing
the converter output frequency. The frequency is reduced down to a minimum value (equaling
twice rated slip).

### p1341[0...n] I_max frequency controller integral time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

### p1400[0...n] Speed control configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6490 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 1000 0000 0010 0001 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the closed-loop speed control.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Automatic Kp/Tn adaptation active | No | Yes | 6040 |
| 01 | Sensorless vector control freeze I comp | No | Yes | 6040 |
| 02 | Acceleration precontrol signal source | Internal (n_set) | External (p1495) | 6031 |
| 03 | Reference model speed setpoint, I component | OFF | ON | 6031 |
| 05 | Kp/Tn adaptation active | No | Yes | 6040 |
| 06 | Free Tn adaptation active | No | Yes | 6050 |
| 07 | Interpolation speed precontrol | No | Yes | 6031 |
| 10 | Speed precontrol | No | Yes | 6031 |
| 14 | Torque precontrol | For n_ctrl enab | Always active | 6060 |
| 15 | Sensorless vector control speed precontrol | No | Yes | 6030 |
| 16 | I component for limiting | Hold | Enable | 6030 |
| 19 | Anti-windup for integral component | No | Yes | 6030 |
| 20 | Acceleration model | OFF | ON | 6031 |
| 24 | Moment of inertia estimator fast estimation active | No | Yes | 6030 |
| 27 | Load gearbox, take into account torque limit | Yes | No | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 01:  
When the bit is set, the I component of the speed controller is kept when changing
into the open-loop controlled mode.  
For bit 16:  
When the bit is set, the integral component of the speed controller is only held if
it reaches the torque limit.  
For bit 19:  
When this bit is set, speed overshoots when accelerating along the torque limit and
for load surges are reduced. If the torque setpoint reaches the torque limit, then
the integral component is set to the difference between the torque limit and the P
component.  
For bit 20:  
The acceleration model for the speed setpoint is only active for encoderless vector
control if p1496 is not zero.  
For bit 24:  
When the bit is set, assuming that the motor accelerates smoothly, the moment of inertia
can be determined faster.

### p1401[0...n] Flux control configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1110 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for flux setpoint control

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Flux setpoint soft starting active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Flux setpoint differentiation active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Flux build-up control active | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Flux characteristic, load-dependent | No | Yes | 6725 |

**Note:**
  
For bit 00 (not for permanent-magnet synchronous motors):  
Initially, the flux is only established with a low rate of rise when magnetizing the
induction motor. The flux setpoint p1570 is reached again at the end of the magnetizing
time p0346.  
For bit 01 (not for permanent-magnet synchronous motors):  
Initially, the flux is only established with a low rate of rise when magnetizing the
induction motor. The flux setpoint p1570 is reached again at the end of the magnetizing
time p0346. When quick magnetizing (p1401.6 = 1) is selected, soft starting is internally
deactivated and alarm A07416 is displayed.  
The flux differentiation can be switched out if a significant ripple occurs in the
field-generating current setpoint (r0075) when entering the field weakening range.
However, this is not suitable for fast acceleration operations because then, the flux
decays more slowly and the voltage limiting responds.  
For bit 02 (not for permanent-magnet synchronous motors):  
The flux build-up control operates during the magnetizing phase p0346 of the induction
motor. If it is switched out, a constant current setpoint is injected and the flux
is built up corresponding to the rotor time constant. When quick magnetizing (p1401.6
= 1) is selected and when flux build-up control is de-energized alarm A07416 is displayed.  
For bit 03:  
The load-dependent calculation of the flux characteristic is only available for separately
excited synchronous motors.

### p1402[0...n] Closed-loop current control and motor model configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the closed-loop control and the motor model.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 02 | Current controller adaptation active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Reluctance torque is used for PEM | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Orientation angle only from the voltage model | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Modified flux setpoint input | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Step-like transition from the U model to the I model orientation | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Enable internally determined down ramp | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Enable residual tachometer | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Enable orientation angle smoothing using PLL | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Enable rotor resistance identification | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Limiting q-component current controller | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Determine FESM rotor position by entering Id (motion-based) | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | FESM rotor position from the incremental encoder with zero track | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
The following bits are effective only in conjunction with a permanent-magnet synchronous
motor.  
For bit 06:  
When the bit is set, in the base speed range, the closed-loop control generates an
additional d-current, which in turn generates an optimum reluctance torque. As a consequence,
the current value can be reduced while still maintaining the same torque. Parameters
p356 (Lq), p357 (Ld) and p316 are used for the calculation.  
  
The following bits are effective only in conjunction with a synchronous machine with
external excitation.  
For bit 07:  
When the bit is set, only the flux angle from the voltage model is used. Under the
condition that the transition from the current model to the voltage model orientation
has been completed.  
For bit 08:  
If the bit is set, the changeover thresholds p1752 and p1756 are compared with the
speed setpoint. The changeover from speed actual value comparison to speed setpoint
comparison can be beneficial - especially for encoderless operation.  
For bit 09:  
If the bit is set, the changeover thresholds p1752 and p1756 are not taken into account
at the transition from the voltage model to the current model orientation. The transition
is then a step-like transition. This can be beneficially used for encoderless operation.  
For bit 10:  
If the bit is set, the down ramp is internally determined for encoderless operation.
The internal down ramp is in this case less or equal to the ramp specified by the
ramp-function generator. This can be practically used for sensorless operation in
conjunction with a diode infeed and active Vdc max controller.  
For bit 11:  
If the bit is set, the speed is determined via the residual tachometer for encoderless
operation and inhibited excitation current unit. This only makes sense when no disconnector
is opened between the converter and the motor in this state.  
For bit 12:  
If this bit is set, smoothing of the orientation angle of the voltage model is enabled
via PLL. The smoothing time can be modified via p6700. The application is recommended
in combination with a sine-wave filter.  
For bit 13:  
When the bit is set, for induction motors the internal rotor time constant is corrected
in the upper speed range.  
For bit 14:  
When the bit is set, for induction motors, modifications are made in the closed-loop
control, which are advantageous when operating with an SL150.  
For bit 15:  
When the bit is set, the Id current is impressed (p1612) for the excited FESM, the
rotating part of the machine (rotor) is deflected.  
For bit 16:  
When the bit is set, the pole position is determined from the encoder with zero track,
the zero pulse position must be determined and saved, p6729 =p6728.

### p1404[0...n] Encoderless operation changeover speed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6070 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 120.00 [%] | 1000.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 1000.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the speed threshold to change over between operation with and without an encoder
(SESM).  
The changeover is only possible when the motor voltage model is active.  
Above this threshold, the drive system is automatically operated in encoderless mode.  
When switching back to operation with encoder, a hysteresis of 10 % applies.  
This threshold is specified as a % referred to p1752.

**Note:**
  
The changeover speed applies when changing over between operation with and without
encoder.  
Separate speed controllers should be set when operating with and without encoder.  
- Operation with encoder: p1460 (Kp), p1462 (Tn), p1461, p1463, p1457, p1458 (speed
controller adaptation)  
- Operation without encoder: p1470 (Kp), p1472 (Tn)  
With p1404 = 1000 %, switchover in encoderless operation is deactivated.  
SESM: separately excited synchronous motor

### p1428[0...n] Speed precontrol balancing dead time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 5030, 5042, 5210 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 | 3.0 | [ 0 ] 0.0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the dead time to symmetrize the speed setpoint for active torque precontrol.  
The selected multiplier refers to the sampling time of the speed controller (dead
time= p1428 * p0115[1]).

**Dependency:**
  
In conjunction with p1429, this parameter can emulate the characteristics of how the
torque is established (dynamic response of closed current control loop).  
  
Refer to:
p1429, p1511

### p1429[0...n] Speed precontrol balancing time constant

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 5030, 5042, 5210, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [ms] | 10000.00 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the time constant (PT1) for symmetrizing the speed setpoint for active torque
precontrol.

**Dependency:**
  
In conjunction with p1428, this parameter can emulate the characteristics of how torque
is established (dynamic response of the closed current control loop).  
For VECTOR (r0107) the following applies:  
The parameter is only effective if the acceleration model is supplied using external
acceleration signals (p1400.2 = 1). For p1400.2 = 0, time constant p1442 (or p1452
for sensorless vector control) is used.  
  
Refer to:
p1428, p1511

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

### r1432[0...1] CO: Speed precontrol

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 5030 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [rpm] | - [rpm] | [ ] - [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the speed precontrol.  
For index [0]:  
Displays the speed precontrol value after symmetrizing for the torque build-up (emulates
the closed current control loop).  
For index [1]:  
Displays the speed precontrol value before the switch p1400.10.  
When the "DSC with spline" function module is activated (r0108.6 = 1, signal source
of p1194.0 = 1), then this is the precontrol value generated by the spline. Otherwise,
this is the value from the signal source of p1430 (possibly after linear interpolation).
The precontrol value generated by the spline is zero if the speed precontrol for the
spline is deactivated (signal source of p1194.4 = 0).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
After symmetrization  
[
1]:
Before switch p1400.10

**Dependency:**
  
Symmetrizing can be parameterized with p1428 and/or p1429.

### p1433[0...n] Speed controller reference model natural frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [Hz] | 8000.0 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the natural frequency of a PT2 element for the reference model of the speed controller.

**Recommend.:**
  
The reference model is correctly set when the characteristics of p1439 (reference
model output) and p1445 (actual speed value) are virtually identical when the I component
of the speed controller is disabled.

**Dependency:**
  
Together with p1434 and p1435, the characteristics (in the time domain) of the closed-loop
speed control (P) can be emulated.  
For VECTOR (r0107) the following applies:  
The reference model is activated with p1400.3 = 1. For sensorless vector control (p1300
= 20) the reference model is disabled in open-loop speed controlled operation (refer
to p1755).  
  
Refer to:
p1434, p1435

### p1434[0...n] Speed controller reference model damping

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 5030, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 | 5.000 | [ 0 ] 1.000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping of a PT2 element for the reference model of the speed controller.

**Recommend.:**
  
The reference model is correctly set when the characteristics of p1439 (reference
model output) and p1445 (actual speed value) are virtually identical when the I component
of the speed controller is disabled.

**Dependency:**
  
In conjunction with p1433 and p1435, the characteristics (in time) of the P-controlled
speed control loop can be emulated.  
For VECTOR (r0107) the following applies:  
The reference model is activated with p1400.3 = 1.  
  
Refer to:
p1433, p1435

### p1435[0...n] Speed controller reference model dead time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 5030, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 | 3.00 | [ 0 ] 0.00 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the "fractional" dead time for the reference model of the speed controller.  
This parameter emulates the computing dead time of the proportionally controlled speed
control loop.  
The selected multiplier refers to the speed controller sampling time (dead time= p1435
* p0115[1]).

**Recommend.:**
  
The reference model is correctly set when the characteristics of p1439 (reference
model output) and p1445 (actual speed value) are virtually identical when the I component
of the speed controller is disabled.

**Dependency:**
  
In conjunction with p1433 and p1434, the characteristics (in time) of the P-controlled
speed control loop can be emulated.  
For VECTOR (r0107) the following applies:  
The reference model is activated with p1400.3 = 1.  
  
Refer to:
p0115, p1433, p1434

### r1436 CO: Speed controller reference model speed setpoint output

| --- | --- | --- |

The reference model is activated with p1400.3 = 1.

### p1437[0...n] CI: Speed controller reference model I component input

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1436[0] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for speed setpoint for the integral component of the speed
controller.

**Dependency:**
  
The reference model is activated with p1400.3 = 1.  
  
Refer to:
p1400

**Notice:**
  
In should be ensured that a speed setpoint is selected as signal source that corresponds
to the setpoint for the P component of the speed controller.

### r1438 CO: Speed controller speed setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 3001, 5019, 5030, 5042, 5210, 6020, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [rpm] | - [rpm] | [ ] - [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the speed setpoint after setpoint limiting for the
P component of the speed controller.  
For U/f operation, the value that is displayed is of no relevance.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1439

**Note:**
  
In the standard state (the reference model is deactivated), r1438 = r1439.

### r1439 Speed setpoint I component

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 5030, 5040, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

### p1440[0...n] CI: Speed controller speed actual value input

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 6040 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 63[0] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the speed actual value of the speed controller.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1443

**Caution:**
  
Speed control with encoder (p1300 = 21):  
For the speed or position signal of the motor model there must always be a motor encoder
available (evaluation via SMC/SMI, see p0400). The actual speed of the motor (r0061)
and the position data for synchronous motors continue to come from this motor encoder
and are not affected by the setting of p1440.  
Interconnection of p1440:  
If connector input p1440 is interconnected with an external speed actual value, the
identical scaling of the speed should be observed (p2000).

**Notice:**
  
If the speed signal is made available via blocks which can be interconnected freely
(DCC), its sampling time should not exceed that of the closed-loop speed controller
(p0115[1]) and the calculation "PRIOR TO closed-loop speed controller" should be made.  
in order that for a motor speed encoder fault, a changeover can be made to encoderless
operation (see p0491), the speed signal, if it is derived from its own motor speed
encoder, must be used from r0063 and not from r0061.

### p1441[0...n] Actual speed smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 4710, 4715 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [ms] | 50.00 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time constant (PT1) for the speed actual value.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0063

**Note:**
  
The speed actual value should be smoothed for encoders with a low pulse number or
for resolvers.  
After this parameter has been changed, we recommend that the speed controller is adapted
and/or the speed controller settings checked Kp (p1460) and Tn (p1462).

### p1442[0...n] Speed controller speed actual value smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6020, 6040 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [ms] | 32000.00 [ms] | [ 0 ] 4.00 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time for the actual speed value of the speed controller for closed-loop
control with encoder.

**Note:**
  
The smoothing must be increased if there is gear backlash. For longer smoothing times,
the integral time of the speed controller must also be increased (e.g. using p0340
= 4).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6040 |

| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |

Displays the speed actual value at the speed controller's free-wiring actual value
input p1440.

### r1444 Speed controller speed setpoint steady-state (static)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 5030 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [rpm] | - [rpm] | [ ] - [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the sum of all speed setpoints that are present.  
The following sources are available for the displayed setpoint:  
- setpoint at the ramp-function generator input (r1119).  
- speed setpoint 1 (p1155).  
- speed setpoint 2 (p1160).  
- speed setpoint for the speed precontrol (p1430).  
- setpoint from DSC (for DSC active).  
- setpoint via PC (for master control active).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1119, p1155, p1160, p1430

### p1456[0...n] Speed controller P gain adaptation lower starting point

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6050 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 400.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower starting point of the adaptation range for the additional adaptation
of the P gain of the speed controller.  
The values are in % and refer to the set source of the adaptation signal.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1455, p1457, p1458, p1459

**Note:**
  
If the upper transition point p1457 of the speed controller adaptation is set to lower
values than the lower transition p1456, then the controller gain below p1457 is adapted
with p1459 and above p1456, with p1458.

### p1460[0...n] Speed controller P gain adaptation speed lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6020, 6040 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

Refer to:
p1461, p1464, p1465

4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the P gain of the speed controller for the upper adaptation speed range (> p1465).  
The entry is made referred to the P gain for the lower adaptation speed range of the
speed controller (% referred to p1460).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1460, p1464, p1465

**Note:**
  
If the upper transition point p1465 of the speed controller adaptation is set to lower
values than the lower transition p1464, then the controller gain below p1465 is adapted
with p1461. This means that an adaptation can be implemented for low speeds without
having to change the controller parameters.

### p1462[0...n] Speed controller integral time adaptation speed lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 5040, 5042, 6020, 6040 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [ms] | 100000.00 [ms] | [ 0 ] 20.00 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the integration time of the speed controller before the adaptation speed range
(0 ... p1464).  
This value corresponds to the basic setting of the integral time of the speed controller
without adaptation (p1461 = 100 %).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1463, p1464, p1465

**Note:**
  
The integral component is stopped if the complete controller output or the sum of
controller output and torque precontrol reach the torque limit.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 01463 - 01622

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 01463 - 01622

4.6

**Note:**
  
If the upper transition point p1465 of the speed controller adaptation is set to lower
values than the lower transition point p1464, then the controller integral time below
p1465 is adapted with p1463. This means that an adaptation can be implemented for
low speeds without having to change the controller parameters.

### p1464[0...n] Speed controller adaptation speed lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6050 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [rpm] | 30000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower adaptation speed of the speed controller.  
No adaptation is effective below this speed.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1460, p1461, p1462, p1463, p1465

**Note:**
  
If the upper transition point p1465 of the speed controller adaptation is set to lower
values than the lower transition point p1464, then the controller below p1465 is adapted
with p1461 or p1463. This means that an adaptation can be implemented for low speeds
without having to change the controller parameters.

### p1465[0...n] Speed controller adaptation speed upper

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6050 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [rpm] | 30000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 30000.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper adaptation speed of the speed controller.  
No adaptation is effective above this speed.  
For the proportional gain, p1460 x p1461 is effective.  
For the integral time, p1462 x p1463 is effective.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1460, p1461, p1462, p1463, p1464

**Note:**
  
If the upper transition point p1465 of the speed controller adaptation is set to lower
values than the lower transition point p1464, then the controller below p1465 is adapted
with p1461 or p1463. This means that an adaptation can be implemented for low speeds
without having to change the controller parameters.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 2520, 5040, 5042, 5210, 6040 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1478, p1479

**Notice:**
  
The parameter may be protected as a result of p0922 or p2079 and cannot be changed.

**Note:**
  
For the interface mode "SIMODRIVE 611 universal" (p2038 = 1), p1477 and p1478 are
used for the signal STW2.6 (integrator inhibit, speed controller).

### p1478[0...n] CI: Speed controller integrator setting value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 6040 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2003 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the integrator setting value for the velocity controller.  
The signal to set this integrator setting value is interconnected via p1477.

**Dependency:**
  
The setting value of the speed controller integrator is weighted with the scaling
factor of the signal source in p1479.  
If p1478 is interconnected to the integral output of the speed controller (r1482),
then after the magnetizing time (r0346) and if the speed controller is enabled, the
integral component of the controller is set to the last value before the pulse inhibit.
This value is set if no setting command (p1477) is interconnected or, at the instant
that the pulses were inhibited, a setting command is available, which is not deactivated
up to the next time that the pulses are inhibited. For sensorless vector control,
in addition p1400.1 should be set to 1 so that when the drive is stopped, the integral
component of the speed controller is not controlled down to zero.  
In order that when setting the integrator output, only the static torque is detected,
we recommend that the accelerating torque is completely precontrolled (e.g. p1496).  
If p1478 is interconnected to another output other than r1482, then after magnetizing
and speed controller enable, the integral output is set once if the setting command
is not interconnected (p1477 = 0).  
  
Refer to:
p1477, p1479

**Notice:**
  
The parameter may be protected as a result of p0922 or p2079 and cannot be changed.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 6030 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

| -2000.0 [%] | 2000.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.0 [%] |
