### p3400[0...n] Converter configuration word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0010 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration word of the converter.  
The type of step-up operation is selected by the combination of bits 0 and 1:  
Bit 1, 0  
= 0, 0 --> stepup with stable DC link voltage  
= 1, 0 --> stepup with DC link capacitor  
= 0, 1 --> stepup with weak DC link voltage

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Step-up type bit 0 | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Step-up type bit 1 | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Capacitor available | C not available | C available | - |
| 03 | Priority at the current limit | I_react has prio | I_active has prio | - |
| 04 | DC current limit | OFF | ON | - |
| 05 | Reserve | OFF | ON | - |
| 06 | Powering up without VSM voltage | OFF | ON | - |
| 07 | Reduce current limit to zero for short-circuit | OFF | ON | - |
| 08 | A load cannot be connected to the DC link voltage | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | Zero sequence comp. shift for bypass on (higher output voltage) | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Reserve | OFF | ON | - |
| 11 | Reserve | OFF | ON | - |
| 12 | Activate extended current limiting controller | OFF | ON | - |
| 13 | Enable current controller in grid droop operation | OFF | ON | - |
| 14 | Reserve | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Switch-on sequence without magnetization | OFF | ON | - |

### r3402 CO: Main sequence control state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2610, 2611, 2612, 2613, 2614, 2615 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 990 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the state of the main sequence control.

**Value:**
  
0:
Not active - POWER ON  
100:
Switching on inhibited  
200:
Ready for switching on  
300:
Switching-on operation  
306:
Cooling  
310:
Disconnector  
320:
Additional auxiliaries  
340:
Motor side switch 1  
370:
Excitation  
375:
Main power supply  
398:
Switching-on operation completed  
400:
Ready  
500:
Operation  
510:
Step-up operation  
515:
Settling operation closed-loop control  
520:
Normal operation  
600:
Stop  
700:
Quick stop  
800:
Start switching-off operation  
820:
Open motor side switch  
830:
Switch off auxiliaries  
840:
Inhibit main power supply  
898:
Switching-off operation completed  
900:
Select test mode  
907:
Test operation auxiliary fan  
910:
Test operation cooling  
912:
Test operation additional auxiliaries  
920:
Test operation motor side switch 1  
924:
Test operation output disconnector 1  
933:
Test operation excitation  
934:
Test operation main power supply  
950:
Test mode switch motor/line supply  
951:
Test mode switch motor/converter  
980:
Test mode power module update  
990:
Test operation circulating operation

### p3480[0...n] Modulation depth limit steady state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 30.0 [%] | 99.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 93.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum steady-state modulation depth.  
When this limit is reached, the total module voltage is boosted. This means that the
control reserve is maintained.

### r3485 Modulation depth reserve controller output

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the reserve controller output for the modulation depth.  
The total cell voltage is increased by this voltage value.  
The summed setpoint for the total cell voltage is output in r0088.  
The summed setpoint is limited to the maximum steady-state total cell voltage (p0280).

### p3520[0...7] CI: Infeed power precontrol signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7603 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for power precontrol.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Power 0  
[
1]:
Power 1  
[
2]:
Power 2  
[
3]:
Power 3  
[
4]:
Power 4  
[
5]:
Power 5  
[
6]:
Power 6  
[
7]:
Power 7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3521

**Note:**
  
Closed-loop control of the DC link voltage is improved by precontrolling the power
required for the other modules. A non-scaled quantity is expected so that the various
power reference values (r2004) of the drive objects do not have to be taken into account.
The scaling factors are used to adapt the scaling (p3521).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3520

### r3522[0...8] CO: DC link precontrol power display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7603 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [kW] | - [kW] | [ ] - [kW] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the resulting precontrol powers.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Power 0  
[
1]:
Power 1  
[
2]:
Power 2  
[
3]:
Power 3  
[
4]:
Power 4  
[
5]:
Power 5  
[
6]:
Power 6  
[
7]:
Power 7  
[
8]:
Total

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3520, p3521

**Note:**
  
These values are used to set the correct power scaling for the relevant precontrol.  
[0]: p3520[0] * p3521[0]  
[1]: p3520[1] * p3521[1]  
[2]: p3520[2] * p3521[2]  
[3]: p3520[3] * p3521[3]  
[4]: p3520[4] * p3521[4]  
[5]: p3520[5] * p3521[5]  
[6]: p3520[6] * p3521[6]  
[7]: p3520[7] * p3521[7]  
[8]: sum [0] ... [7]

### r3535[0...3] CO: Infeed active current limit display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7603 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Arms] | - [Arms] | [ ] - [Arms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
For vector, the parameter has no significance. The limits scaled with the referred
line voltage (only the positive phase sequence system component) are indicated in
indices 2 and 3. These can be used as current limits for additional converters.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Active current limit positive  
[
1]:
Active current limit negative  
[
2]:
Positive active current limit multiplied by the line voltage  
[
3]:
Negative active current limit multiplied by the line voltage

### p3782 BI: External synchronoscope frequency higher signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the "Raise frequency" command of the external synchronoscope.  
When BI: p3781 = 1 signal (operation) the commands "raise frequency" or "lower frequency"
are evaluated as follows:  
BI: p3782 = 0, BI: p3783 = 0 --> frequency remains unchanged  
BI: p3782 = 1, BI: p3783 = 0 --> frequency is raised  
BI: p3782 = 0, BI: p3783 = 1 --> frequency is lowered  
BI: p3782 = 1, BI: p3783 = 1 --> frequency is lowered

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3783, r3803

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

4.8

Sets the signal source for the "Raise voltage" command of the external synchronoscope.  
When BI: p3781 = 1 signal (operation) the commands "raise voltage" or "lower voltage"

BI: p3784 = 1, BI: p3785 = 0 --> raise voltage

### p3787 BI: External synchronoscope inhibit signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the "Lock" message of the external synchronoscope.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3803  
Refer to:
A07947, F07948

**Note:**
  
Inhibit in the basic state (BI: p3781 = 0 signal):  
Alarm A07947 is output.  
Inhibit outside the basic state (BI: p3781 = 1 signal):  
Fault F07948 is initiated, and the synchronoscope is "shed".

### p3788[0...n] Synchronoscope starting frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -65.0 [Hz] | 5.0 [Hz] | [ 0 ] -1.0 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the difference to the line frequency.  
If the actual output frequency is greater than the sum of the rated line frequency
and this setting value, then drive control is relinquished to the internal or the
external synchronoscope.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3803

### p3792[0...n] Synchronoscope monitoring time synchronism

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [s] | 180 [s] | [ 0 ] 10 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time for the "Synchronism reached" message in parameter r3803.6
or 14.  
After starting the internal or external synchronoscope, within this time "synchronism
reached" must be achieved.  
The setting is valid for the internal and the external synchronoscope.

r3803

F07948

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

(r3803.9).  
The setting is valid for the internal synchronoscope.

Refer to:
r3803

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

### r3797 CO: Synchronoscope output frequency

### p3800 Synchronoscope mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the synchronoscope mode.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal synchronoscope selection | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | External synchronoscope selection | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | External synchronoscope direct control circuit breaker | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Synchronoscope test mode | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 00, 01:  
It is not permissible to simultaneously select the internal synchronoscope and the
external synchronoscope.

### r3803.0...26 CO/BO: Synchronoscope control word/status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and binector output for the control word/status word

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Synchronoscope enable active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Synchronoscope start frequency reached | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Synchronoscope test mode | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Internal synchronoscope is selected | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Internal synchronoscope is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Internal synchronoscope synchronism reached | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Internal synchronoscope error active | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Internal synchronoscope frequency within bandwidth | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Internal synchronoscope angle within bandwidth | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Internal synchronoscope voltage within bandwidth | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | External synchronoscope selected | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | External synchronoscope active | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | External synchronoscope synchronism reached | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | External synchronoscope faulted | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | External synchronoscope inhibited | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | External synchronoscope frequency higher | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | External synchronoscope frequency lower | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | External synchronoscope voltage higher | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | External synchronoscope voltage lower | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | External synchronoscope start | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | External synchronoscope stop | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | External synchronoscope select function group 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | External synchronoscope select function group 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | External synchronoscope test synchronization | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | External synchronoscope direct control circuit breaker | No | Yes | - |

### r3805 CO: Synchronoscope frequency difference

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Hz] | - [Hz] | [ ] - [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the frequency difference between the target voltage (= line supply voltage)
and the output voltage.

### p3806[0...n] Synchronoscope frequency synchronous check

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.02 [Hz] | 1.00 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 0.10 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible frequency difference between the target voltage (= line voltage)
and output voltage for a synchronous check.  
The "Frequency in synchronism" condition is satisfied if the difference (r3805) lies
within this setting.  
When using the internal synchronoscope, this is indicated in parameter r3803.8.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3803, r3805

### p3811[0...n] Synchronoscope start frequency synchronous check

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -1.0 [Hz] | -0.1 [Hz] | [ 0 ] -0.5 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the starting frequency of the output voltage for the synchronous check.  
The start frequency is obtained from the sum of the target frequency (= line frequency)
and this setting value.

### p3813[0...n] Synchronoscope phase synchronous check

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.2 [°] | 5.0 [°] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible phase difference between the target voltage (= line voltage)
and output voltage for a synchronous check.  
The "Phases in synchronism" condition is satisfied if the difference (r3808) lies
within this setting.  
When using the internal synchronoscope, this is indicated in parameter r3803.9.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3803, r3808

### p3815[0...n] Synchronoscope voltage synchronous check

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Tech_ctrl) | **P-Group:**Applications | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

Sets the permissible difference between the amplitude of the target voltage (= line
voltage) and output voltage for a synchronous check.  
The "Voltage in synchronism" condition is satisfied if the difference (r3814) lies
within this setting.  
When using the internal synchronoscope, this is indicated in parameter r3803.10.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3803, r3814

### r3840.0...8 CO/BO: Friction characteristic status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the friction characteristic.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Friction characteristic OK | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Record friction characteristic activated | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Record friction characteristic completed | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Record friction characteristic canceled | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Friction characteristic positive direction | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
Bit 0 = 1:  
All friction characteristic values (p3820 ... p3839) of all existing drive data sets
are plausible.

### p3842 Friction characteristic activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate and deactivate the friction characteristic.

**Value:**
  
0:
Friction characteristic deactivated  
1:
Friction characteristic activated

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1569, r3841, p3845

**Notice:**
  
In order that the friction characteristic can be activated, all friction characteristic
values (p3820 ... p3839) of all existing drive data sets must be plausible.  
For speed values the following applies:  
0.0 < p3820 < p3821 < ... < p3829 <= p0322 or p1082, if p0322 = 0  
For torque values, the following applies:  
0 <= p3830, p3831 ... p3839 <= r0333

### p3845 Record friction characteristic activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting for the friction characteristic record.  
After the next switch-on command, the friction characteristic is automatically recorded.

**Value:**
  
0:
Record friction characteristic deactivated  
1:
Record friction characteristic activated all directions  
2:
Record friction characteristic activated positive direction  
3:
Record friction characteristic activated negative direction

**Dependency:**
  
When selecting the friction characteristic measurement, the drive data set changeover
is suppressed.  
For linear drives (refer to r0108.12) it is not permissible to carry out the friction
characteristic measurement for mechanical systems that limit travel.

**Danger:**
  
For drives with a mechanical system that limit the distance moved, it must be ensured
that during recording, the friction characteristic is not reached. If this is not
the case, then it is not permissible that the measurement is carried out.

**Notice:**
  
To permanently accept the determined settings they must be saved in a non-volatile
fashion (p0971, p0977).

**Note:**
  
When the friction characteristic record is active, it is not possible to save the
parameters (p0971, p0977).  
When the friction characteristic record is active (p3845 > 0), it is not possible
to change p3820 ... p3829, p3830 ... p3839 and p3842.  
When recording the friction characteristic, in addition to the friction, the motor
losses are also determined (e.g. iron losses, eddy current losses and re-magnetizing
losses). A differentiation is not made between these individual loss components. We
recommend that a motor temperature sensor is used because torque deviations can also
be emulated/mapped on the characteristic due to the thermal influence.

### p3846[0...n] Record friction characteristic ramp-up/ramp-down time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 999999.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 10.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the ramp-up/ramp-down time of the ramp-up/ramp-down function generator to automatically
Documenting the friction characteristic.  
The drive is accelerated from standstill (setpoint = 0) up to the maximum speed/velocity
(p1082) in this time.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3845

### p3847[0...n] Record friction characteristic time to warm up

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 3600.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the warm-up time.  
To start the automatic recording, the highest selected speed (p3829) is approached
and this time is held. After this, the measurement is started with the highest speed.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3829, p3845

### p3848[0...n] CI: Friction characteristic speed actual value signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 63[0] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the speed actual value of the friction characteristic.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1443

**Notice:**
  
The interconnected signal of the speed actual value must be approximately the same
as the real motor speed so that when the motor model is replaced, no significant settling
operations occur.

### p3870 Long stator configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration when operating a long stator motor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate long stator help functions | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | Suppress Gx_ZSW.14 | Inactive | Active | - |

**Notice:**
  
The following restrictions apply to this function:  
- it is not permissible to change over the drive data set.  
- the encoder/drive may not be parked using a PROFIBUS telegram.  
- a maximum of 4 drives may be connected to the Control Unit.  
- it is not permissible to commutate with the zero mark (p0404).

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
All of the help functions for long stator motors can be enabled/disabled using this
bit.  
For bit 01:  
When the bit is set, bit 14 (parking encoder active) is set to 0 in the encoder status
word GX_ZSW independent of whether the encoder is parked or not.

### p3895[0...n] Current limiting controller response threshold dynamic

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.000 [%] | 500.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the dynamic response threshold of the short-circuit current limiting  
If the absolute current increases within a sampling time by the percentage value entered
here, then the short-circuit current limiting intervenes.  
The percentage value refers to the rated converter current (r0207).

Refer to:
p3890, p3891

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 | 100000.000 | [ 0 ] 0.000 |

current.  
This value is automatically pre-set using p3900 or p0340 when commissioning has been
completed.

### p3900 Completion of quick commissioning

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Exits quick commissioning (p0010 = 1) with automatic calculation of all parameters
of all existing drive data sets that depend on the entries made during quick commissioning.  
p3900 = 1 initially includes a parameter reset (factory setting, the same as p0970
= 1) for all parameters of the drive object; however, without overwriting the entries
made during the quick commissioning.  
The interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram selection (p0922) and the interconnections
via p0700, p1000 and p1500 are re-established and all of the dependent motor, open-loop
and control-loop control parameters are calculated (corresponding to p0340 = 1).  
p3900 = 2 includes the restoration of the interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram
selection (p0922) and the interconnections via p0700, p1000 and p1500 and the calculations
corresponding to p0340 = 1.  
p3900 = 3 only includes the calculations associated with the motor, open-loop and
closed-loop control parameters corresponding to p0340 = 1.

**Value:**
  
0:
No quick parameterization  
1:
Quick parameterization after parameter reset  
2:
Quick parameterization (only) for BICO and motor parameters  
3:
Quick parameterization for motor parameters (only)

**Notice:**
  
After the value has been modified, no further parameter modifications can be made
and the status is shown in r3996. Modifications can be made again when r3996 = 0.

**Note:**
  
When the calculations have been completed, p3900 and p0010 are automatically reset
to a value of zero.  
When calculating motor, open-loop and closed-loop control parameters (such as for
p0340 = 1) parameters associated with a selected Siemens catalog motor are not overwritten.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 03925 - 04961

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 03925 - 04961

### r3925[0...n] Identification final display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**SESM, REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the commissioning steps that have been carried out.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Motor/control parameters calculated (p0340 = 1, p3900 > 0) | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Motor data identification carried out at standstill (p1910 = 1) | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Rotating measurement carried out (p1960 = 1, 2) | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Automatic parameterization as Standard Drive Control | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Automatic parameterization as Dynamic Drive Control | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | First motor commissioning | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Equivalent circuit diagram parameters changed | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Cable resistance measured | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Circle identification executed | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
The individual bits are only set if the appropriate action has been initiated and
successfully completed.  
When motor rating plate parameters are changed, the final display is reset.

### r3979 BICO counter drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

### p3981 Acknowledge drive object faults

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8060 |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to acknowledge all active faults of a drive object.

**Notice:**
  
Safety messages cannot be acknowledged using this parameter.

**Note:**
  
Parameter should be set from 0 to 1 to acknowledge.  
After acknowledgment, the parameter is automatically reset to 0.

### p3985 Master control mode selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to change over the master control / LOCAL mode.

**Value:**
  
0:
Change master control for STW1.0 = 0  
1:
Change master control in operation

**Danger:**
  
When changing the master control in operation, the drive can manifest undesirable
behavior - e.g. it can accelerate up to another setpoint.

### p3990[0...n] Sequentially resets drive parameters

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 300 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Parameter to internally calculate subsequent (follow-on) parameters after a factory
setting was carried out.  
The parameter is then reset back to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Start a parameter reset  
3:
Start download of volatile parameters from RAM  
5:
Starts a safety parameter reset  
6:
Start reset non-safety/safety parameters  
10:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 10  
11:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 11  
12:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 12  
30:
Start loading delivery condition saved with p0971 = 30  
100:
Start a BICO interconnection reset  
300:
Only Siemens internal

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is called when first commissioning the system and also for factory
settings if this has been initiated via p0970 or p3900.  
  
Refer to:
p0970, p3900

### r3996[0...1] Parameter write inhibit status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays whether writing to parameters is inhibited.  
r3996[0] = 0:  
Parameter write not inhibited.  
0 < r3996[0] < 100:  
Parameter write inhibited. The value shows how the calculations are progressing.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Progress calculations  
[
1]:
Cause

**Note:**
  
For index [1]:  
Only for internal Siemens troubleshooting.

### r4021[0...n] CO: PSA4 digital inputs terminal actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Displays the actual value of the digital inputs/outputs of the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA4).  
This means that the actual input signal can be checked at terminal DI x prior to switching
from the simulation mode (p4095.x = 1) to the terminal mode (p4095.x = 0).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) gate supply voltage (GSV) faulted | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | Yes | No | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) | Low | High | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Low | High | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Low | High | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Low | High | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### r4047[0...n] PSA4 digital outputs status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the digital outputs of Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command circuit breaker undervoltage coil | Yes | No | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) enable circuit breaker | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) protective breaker GSV tripped | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) protective breaker anticondens. heating tripped | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4028, p4048

**Note:**
  
Inversion using p4048 has been taken into account.  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The significance of the outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4048[0...n] PSA4 invert digital outputs

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the signals at the digital outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Invert DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Invert DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Invert DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Invert DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Invert DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 16 | Invert DI/DO 0 (X321.13) protective breaker GSV tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 17 | Invert DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 18 | Invert DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 19 | Invert DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 20 | Invert DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 21 | Invert DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 22 | Invert DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 23 | Invert DI/DO 7 (X321.3) prot. breaker anti-cond. heating tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Note:**
  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.  
When the signal is configured as input, then the associated bit in p4048 has no function.

### p4095[0...n] PSA4 digital inputs simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the simulation mode for the digital inputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |

**Dependency:**
  
The setpoint for the input signals is specified using p4096.  
  
Refer to:
p4096

**Note:**
  
This parameter is not saved when data is backed-up (p0971, p0977).  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4096[0...n] PSA4 digital inputs simulation mode setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint of the input signals in the simulation mode for the digital inputs
of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Low | High | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Low | High | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
The simulation of a digital input is selected using p4095.  
  
Refer to:
p4095

**Note:**
  
This parameter is not saved when data is backed-up (p0971, p0977).  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4630[0...n] Absolute encoder linear measuring step factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 | 4294967295 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the resolution of the absolute position for a linear absolute encoder as factor
from p0407.

**Note:**
  
The serial protocol of an absolute encoder provides the position with a certain resolution
(e.g. 100 nm).  
The resolution is calculated from p0407/p4630.

### p4642 Encoder fault test function

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 255 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Test function to initiate an encoder fault  
For p4642 = 1:  
When setting, an encoder fault is output once at the currently used motor encoder.
Depending on the configuration in p0491, the drive switches over to encoderless operation
This means that encoderless motor stopping can be tested.  
For p4642 = 2:  
When setting, an encoder fault is output once at the currently used motor encoder.
For braking, the drive still uses the commutation position of the encoder. This means
that a faster motor stopping with encoder can be tested (only functions with SMx modules,
otherwise the same response as for p4642 = 1).  
The following generally applies:  
After acknowledgment, the encoder fault that was issued is cleared and p4642 is set
= 0.

**Notice:**
  
It is not permissible that a data set is switched over between an encoder fault being
issued and its deletion.

### r4648.0 CO/BO: Encoder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the encoder status word.  
For bit 00:  
The bit is a group message for all encoders of the axis, and is set for the following
alarms:  
A3x407: "Function limit reached"  
A3x415: "Signal level track A or B out of tolerance (alarm)"  
A3x419: "Track A or B out of tolerance"  
A3x442: "Battery voltage alarm threshold reached"  
x = encoder 1, 2, 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Encoder maintenance required | No | Yes | - |

**Notice:**
  
Maintenance work may have to be carried out on the encoder.

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
For test purposes, the bit can be set using p4642 = 3.  
The bit is reset using p4642 = 0.

### p4649[0...n] Encoder function reserve amplitude limit incremental signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 500 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Amplitude threshold of the incremental signals for the function reserve.  
If the set amplitude threshold on the incremental signals is fallen below, then alarm
A3x407 "Encoder x; function limit reached " is output.

**Note:**
  
An amplitude threshold of 230 mV can be used as default value.

### p4652[0...2] XIST1_ERW reset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4750 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to reset the actual value in XIST_ERW (CO: r4653).

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Reset with zero mark  
2:
Reset with BICO  
3:
Reset with selected zero mark

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4653, r4654, p4655

**Note:**
  
The absolute value is only valid after passing the zero mark.  
For value = 1:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset when passing every zero mark.  
For value = 2:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset with a 0/1 edge via binector input p4655.  
If value = 3:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset after a 0/1 edge via binector input p4655 when passing
the next zero mark.

### p4660[0...2] Sensor Module filter bandwidth

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [kHz] | 20000.00 [kHz] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [kHz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the filter bandwidth for Sensor Module SMx10 (resolver) and SMx20 (sin/cos).  
The value set on the Sensor Module is displayed in r4661.  
Currently, the Sensor Module hardware only supports the following values:  
- 0: The Sensor Module's default setting is used.  
- 50 kHz  
- 170 kHz  
- 500 kHz  
- Unlimited: Only the bandwidth of the operational amplifier is effective.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4661

**Note:**
  
A value of zero is displayed if an encoder is not present.

### p4662[0...n] Encoder characteristic type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the characteristic type.  
For non-linear sensors, the interrelationship between the signal voltage and the position
can be defined using a third degree polynomial.

**Value:**
  
0:
Characteristic inactive  
1:
Characteristic polynomial third degree

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4663, p4664, p4665, p4666

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
A third degree polynomial is defined as follows:  
F(x) = K3 * x^3 + K2 * x^2 + K1 * x + K0  
Coefficients K0 ... K3 should be defined and entered into p4663 ... p4666.  
The sensor range is emulated to x = -0.5 ... +0.5.

### p4670[0...n] Analog sensor configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for evaluation on the analog sensor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 06 | Set velocity to 0 | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Position value range | -0.5 / +0.5 pulse | 0.0 / 1.0 pulse | - |
| 09 | Fault/alarm messages | Fault | Alarm | - |
| 10 | Channel B act | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Channel A act | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Commutation angle constant | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Suppress faults | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Two-channel evaluation | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Extrapolation | OFF | ON | - |

**Notice:**
  
For bit 06:  
Setting the bit sets the velocity actual value (r0061) permanently to 0.  
For bit 13:  
Setting the bit sets the commutation angle permanently to the commutation angle offset
(p0431).

**Note:**
  
For bit 09:  
A setting of bit = 0 will trigger a fault for the relevant channel if the actual value
is invalid.  
A setting of bit = 1 will trigger an alarm for the relevant channel if the actual
value is invalid.  
Zu Bit 10, 11:  
If both channels are activated, the actual value is generated from the average value
of both channels. If a channel fails (actual value invalid), it is not included when
the average value is generated.  
For bit 14:  
The bit is only evaluated for encoder 1. Otherwise no effect.  
For bit 15:  
When the bit is set, both input signals are separately evaluated and transferred.  
Prerequisite:  
Function "Extended cyclic DRIVE-CLiQ telegram" is activated (p0454.4 = 1).

### p4671[0...n] Analog sensor input

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the input circuit for the analog sensor.

**Value:**
  
0:
Differential  
1:
Single-ended A, B  
2:
Single-ended A*, B*  
3:
Single-ended A, B sensitive

**Note:**
  
p4671 = 0:  
The two signals on a track are evaluated differentially.  
p4671 = 1:  
Only the non-inverted signal on a track is evaluated.  
p4671 = 2:  
Only the inverted signal on a track is evaluated.  
p4671 = 3:  
Only the non-inverted signal on a track (high resolution) is evaluated.

### p4672[0...n] Analog sensor channel A voltage at actual value zero

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10.0000 [V] | 10.0000 [V] | [ 0 ] 0.0000 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the voltage when the connected sensor is at actual value zero.  
At this voltage channel A supplies an actual value of zero.

### p4673[0...n] Analog sensor channel A voltage per encoder period

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10.0000 [V] | 10.0000 [V] | [ 0 ] 6.0000 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the output voltage range to be mapped for the connected analog sensor.  
The voltage range is determined by the following parameters:  
- p4672 (voltage at actual value 0)  
- p4673 (voltage per encoder period)

**Note:**
  
The minimum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4672 - p4673/2.  
The maximum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4672 + p4673/2.

### p4680[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance permissible

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible tolerance in encoder pulses for the zero mark distance in the
context of zero mark monitoring.  
Causes fault F3x100 to appear less frequently.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F31100

### p4681[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window limit 1 positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 1 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
The pulse number is corrected if the deviation is less than this limit. If it is higher
than this limit, fault F3x131 is triggered.  
If fault F3x131 is re-parameterized to an alarm (A) or no message (N), then the encoder
pulses that are not corrected are transferred to the accumulator (p4688). The accumulator
can be deactivated using p0437.7.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131

**Note:**
  
This monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
The positive limit describes additional pulses due to EMC.

### p4682[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window limit 1 negative

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -1001 | 0 | [ 0 ] -1001 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the negative tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 1 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
The pulse number is corrected if the deviation is less than this limit. If it is higher
than this limit, fault F3x131 is triggered.  
If fault F3x131 is re-parameterized to an alarm (A) or no message (N), then the encoder
pulses that are not corrected are transferred to the accumulator (p4688). The accumulator
can be deactivated using p0437.7.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4681, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131

**Note:**
  
This monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
For a set value = -1001, the negated value of p4681 becomes active.  
The negative limit describes the pulses lost due to a covered glass panel in the incremental
encoder.

### p4683[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window alarm threshold positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 2 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
Accumulator (p4688) is compared with this parameter, and where relevant, alarm A3x422
is output for 5 seconds.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4681, p4682, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131, A31422

**Note:**
  
Zero mark monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).

### p4685[0...n] Speed actual value average value generation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |

### p4686[0...n] Zero mark minimum length

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |

p0425, p0437

< p0425).  
The parameter is activated using p0437.1 = 1 (zero mark edge detection).

### r4689[0...2] CO: Squarewave encoder diagnostics

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the encoder status according to PROFIdrive for a squarewave encoder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A31422

**Note:**
  
After alarm A3x422 is output, this parameter is set for 100 ms.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**r4951 | **Func. diagram:** - |

Displays the identifier of the Technology Extensions installed on this drive object.  
r4955[0...8]: Identifier of Technology Extension 1  
r4955[9...17]: Identifier of Technology Extension 2, ...

Refer to:
r4950, r4951, r4952, p4956, r4957, r4958, r4959, r4960

**Notice:**
  
This parameter is only indexed if at least one drive object-specific Technology Extension
exists (p4950 > 0).

**Note:**
  
TEC: Technology Extension

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

4.7

0:
Technology Extension inactive  
1:
Technology Extension active

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 05434 - 06562

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 05434 - 06562

### p5434 Direct component controller low-pass limit frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.100 [Hz] | 200.000 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 50.000 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the limit frequency for the low-pass filter to determine the DC components.

### p5436 Direct component controller P gain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0000 [ohm] | 10.0000 [ohm] | [ 0 ] 0.0200 [ohm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the proportional gain for the PI controller to suppress the direct component
in the converter current.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5437

**Note:**
  
Recommended setting: p5436 = total resistance of reactor and transformer.  
The parameter is preset when selecting the power unit (p201). In so doing, as approximation,
a transformer with the same rating and a total power loss of 1.5 % is assumed:  
p5436 = 1.5 % * r0206[0] / (3 * r0207[0] * r0207[0])

### p5437 Direct component controller integration time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 100.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 3.500 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the integration time for the PI controller to suppress the direct component in
the converter current.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5436

**Note:**
  
The parameter is preset when selecting the power unit (p201):  
p5437 = p5492 / p5436

### p5438 Direct component controller limiting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 10.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the controller output voltage limiting for the direct component controller.  
This value is entered as a percentage of the rated voltage (p0210).

### p5956 Automatic restart speed threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6762, 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 120.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 50.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the speed threshold for the automatic restart or buffering brief power failures.  
For the correspondingly parameterized (p1210) and enabled function (p1208[3]), the
following applies:  
- the function is executed above this speed threshold.  
- the function is not executed or a function that is running is interrupted below
this speed threshold.

**Note:**
  
The monitoring is deactivated with p9556 = 0 %.

### p5957 Excitation completed flux threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**ASM, PMSM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 85.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the actual flux threshold of the separately excited synchronous motor (SESM).  
Above this threshold, the SESM is considered to have been excited.

**Notice:**
  
The factory setting should only be changed in exceptional cases.

**Note:**
  
SESM: separately excited synchronous motor

### p5990 Current model test mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6727 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Selects the test mode for the current model to check and optimize the current model
stability.  
The stability of the current model is tested in the test mode with the pulses inhibited.
The expected values for the reference (setpoint) flux and the field-generating current
are set for the corresponding operating point. During the test, the torque-generating
current is increased up to the maximum value in steps.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5991, p5992, p5993

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test operation.
