### p6026[0...7] BI: PU door release enable signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9827 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0...6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the electromagnetic door release enable.  
The signals that are read in are ANDed with the internal states such as 'U=0', 'pulse
inhibit' and 'circuit breaker open' and output at Power Stack Adapter output DO3.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Internal 1  
[
1]:
Internal 2  
[
2]:
Internal 3  
[
3]:
External 1  
[
4]:
External 2  
[
5]:
External 3  
[
6]:
Static  
[
7]:
Temporary

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6027

### p6027 PU door release enable signals signal level

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9827 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1111 1111 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal level to evaluate for the signal sources of the door release enable.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal enable 1 | Low | High | - |
| 01 | Internal enable 2 | Low | High | - |
| 02 | Internal enable 3 | Low | High | - |
| 03 | External enable 1 | Low | High | - |
| 04 | External enable 2 | Low | High | - |
| 05 | External enable 3 | Low | High | - |
| 06 | Static enable 1 | Low | High | - |
| 07 | Temporary enable 1 | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6026

**Note:**
  
The bit positions correspond to the index of parameter p6026.

### r6028.0...31 CO/BO: PU: Door release enable signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9827 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the enable signals for the door release.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Switching on inhibited or switching on ready and no ON command | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Circuit breaker open | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | DC link voltage = 0 | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Output voltage=0 | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Cell controls failed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Discharge time expired | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Internal enable 0 ... 14 total | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Enable 1 (p6026.0) | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Enable 2 (p6026.1) | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Enable 3 (p6026.2) | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | External enable 1 (p6026.3) | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | External enable 2 (p6026.4) | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | External enable 3 (p6026.5) | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | Static enable (p6026.6) | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Temporary enable (p6026.7) | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Internal and external enable 0 ... 21 total | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Enable 0 ... 29 total | No | Yes | - |

### r6044 CO: PU I2t t_remain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [s] | - [s] | [ ] - [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for remaining time of the I2t power unit monitoring.  
The remaining time is that time calculated by the I2t model until 100% thermal utilization
is reached (r6045). The time depends on the actual current as well as the utilization
level.  
Operation below the permissible rated current -> r6044 = 3600 s  
Operation above the permissible rated current -> r6044 < 3600 s

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6045

**Note:**
  
Example:  
Operation with the permissible rated current (1110 A)  
-> r6044 = 3600 s  
The current increases and remains approximately constant (1110 ... 1120 A)  
-> r6044 = 55 s (fluctuating between 25 and 200 s), r6045 = approx. 99 %  
Current increases further  
-> r6044 = 0 s, r6045 = 100 %, shutdown (F30005)

### p6060[0...1] BI: External command switch in power unit cooling signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORGL, VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the external command "switch on power unit cooling".  
BI: p6060 = 0 signal  
If there is no internal switch-on signal, then the power unit cooling is switched
off.  
Exceptions:  
- when the run-on time (p0264) is appropriately set.  
- when fault F49156 occurs.  
BI: p6060 = 1 signal  
The drive converter cooling is switched on.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Redundancy subsystem 1  
[
1]:
Redundancy subsystem 2

**Note:**
  
For redundancy, the cooling units of both subsystems are switched on using their own
index

### r6071.0...6 CO/BO: PU cell bypass status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the bypass switch of a double submodule
(DSM).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bypass enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Component with bypass | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Bypass activated | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Bypass deselected | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Bypass voltage monitoring active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | All cells fault-free power unit 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | All cells fault-free power unit 2 | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6070  
Refer to:
A30206

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
Bypass is activated in the power unit description data.  
For bit 01:  
Only components with bypass connection board are installed This display should only
be evaluated if a voltage is available at the cells.  
For bit 02:  
Bypass functionality is selected using parameter p6070, and is activated in the power
unit description data.  
For bit 03:  
Bypass functionality is deselected using parameter p6070, and is activated in the
power unit description data (see A30206).  
For bit 04:  
Is not used  
For bit 05:  
All double submodules are fault-free

### p6112 Bypass DSM number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 9999 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Selection to set the bypass response of a double submodule (DSM).

**Note:**
  
The numbering of the DSM is identical to that in the alarm value of alarm A30209.

### p6113[0...n] Bypass DSM marking

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Setting to mark a double submodule as bypassed or not bypassed.  
The value for the DSM specified in p6112 is displayed.  
1 corresponds to bypassed  
0 corresponds to not bypassed

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6078, r6079, r6080, p6112

**Note:**
  
Before changing the bypass marking, the DSM number must be entered into p6112.  
If the DSM is marked as being bypassed (r6078, r6079, r6080), then by entering a change
of the value from 1 to 0, it can be marked as being not bypassed – and vice versa.

| 00 | Switch on cooling | OFF | ON | 9795 |
| 01 | Internal output disconnector close | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Power circuit switch on excitation | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | Close output circuit breaker | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Switch on input transformer premagnetization | OFF | ON | - |
| 18 | Enable door release | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Enable automatic offset calculation | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Switch on additional auxiliaries | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | Enable infeed | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Enable DC link monitoring | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | DSM cell update switch-on precharging | OFF | ON | - |
| 28 | Close switch circulating operation | OFF | ON | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6650

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Converter cooling switched on | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Output disconnector closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Power circuit excitation switched on | OFF | ON | - |
| 04 | Test operation active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Main infeed enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Additional auxiliaries switched on | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Output side circuit breaker closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Converter operation | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Stop active | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Quick stop active | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Converter switch off active | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | External converter enable | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | Fault with stop response OFF1 is present | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Fault with stop response OFF2 is present | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | Fault with stop response OFF3 is present | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | Fault with stop response OFF1_DELAYED is present | No | Yes | - |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the breaker.  
For p6230[x] = 1, the following applies:  
The simulation mode is automatically deactivated (p6670[y] = 0 signal).  
For p6230[x] = 0, the following applies:  
The simulation mode is automatically activated (p6670[y] = 1 signal).

**Value:**
  
0:
Breaker not present  
1:
Breaker present

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Circuit breaker  
[
1]:
Disconnector

### p6243[0...n] PolID motion-based alignment duration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [s] | 15 [s] | [ 0 ] 1 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the wait time when aligning the rotor during motion-based rotor position identification.  
After the motor excitation has built up and the Id current has been specified, the
ramp-function generator enable is delayed by this time.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0346, p1402, p1612

| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -20000.00 [rpm] | 20000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**

### p6278[0...n] Reverse field excitation speed setp. rot. field inv. hysteresis

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -20000.00 [rpm] | 20000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the hysteresis of the speed setpoint for rotating field inversion of the stator
current in the reverse field exciter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6277

**Note:**
  
The amount of the value entered in the parameter is dynamically limited to the rated
speed of the motor.  
The value 0 is not permissible.  
The sign of the hysteresis defines the rotating field of the stator current for the
reverse field exciter depending on the mechanical direction of rotation.  
The hysteresis is symmetrical around the value in parameter p6277.

### r6279.13...15 CO/BO: Flux monitoring and speed monitoring state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the flux monitoring and speed monitoring.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 13 | Mech. direction of rotation from reverse field excitation | Turns anti-clockw. | Turns clockwise | - |
| 14 | Torque-generating abs current setpoint value less than threshold | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Torque-generating Iset * f is positive | Negative | Positive | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0063

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
The threshold value is set in p6270.  
For bit 01:  
The threshold value is set in p6272.  
For bit 03:  
The threshold value is set in p1226.  
For bit 04:  
The threshold value is set in p1752.  
For bit 05:  
The threshold value is set in p1404.  
For bit 07, 14:  
The threshold value is set in p6276.  
For bit 08:  
The threshold value is set in p6280.  
For bit 09:  
The threshold value is set in p6281.  
For bit 10:  
The threshold value is set in p6282.  
For bit 11:  
The threshold value is set in p6283.  
For bit 13:  
The threshold value is set in p6278.

4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the infeed test.  
BI: p6289 = 1 signal:  
- test operation is switched on.  
- p6650 is automatically set to 34 (test operation, main infeed).  
BI: p6289 = 0 signal:  
- test operation is switched-off.  
- p6650 is automatically set to 0.

### p6296[0...2] Cooling unit water flow thresholds

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9798 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -75.00 [ltr/min] | 375.00 [ltr/min] | [ 0 ] -1.00 [ltr/min]  [ 1 ] 160.00 [ltr/min]  [ 2 ] 5.00 [ltr/min] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the thresholds for monitoring the water flow.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Water flow plausibility threshold  
[
1]:
Water flow alarm threshold  
[
2]:
Water flow hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the equipment description
data.  
For index [0]:  
If the flux monitoring is active (fault threshold (device description data) ! =0),
the following applies to the plausibility threshold setting:  
Wire breakage threshold (equipment description data) < plausibility threshold < -1
liter.  
If the plausibility threshold is set lower than the wire breakage threshold, then
the value is limited to the wire breakage threshold.  
For index [1]:  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Hysteresis < alarm threshold > fault threshold (equipment description data) + hysteresis  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold + hysteresis, then the
value is limited to the fault threshold + hysteresis.  
For index [2]:  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
0 < hysteresis < 20 liters

### p6298[0...1] Cooling unit differential pressure min value alarm thresh

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9799 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [bar] | 10.0 [bar] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [bar]  [ 1 ] 0.3 [bar] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold/hysteresis for the minimum differential pressure.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Liquid differential pressure alarm threshold  
[
1]:
Liquid differential pressure hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Alarm threshold > fault threshold (hardware description data)  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold, then the value is limited
to the fault threshold (from the hardware description data) + hysteresis (p6298.1).  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the hardware description
data.  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
The hysteresis must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 6 bar.

### p6299[0...1] Power unit cooling input temperature alarm threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9797 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV, B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit, Fan), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 40.0 [°C]  [ 1 ] 1.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for the maximum intake temperature for liquid cooling or
intake temperature for air cooling.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature alarm threshold  
[
1]:
Temperature hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the equipment description
data.  
For index [0]:  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
20 °C < alarm threshold < fault threshold (equipment description data) - hysteresis.  
If the alarm threshold is set higher than the fault threshold - hysteresis, then the
value is limited to the fault threshold - hysteresis.  
For index [1]:  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
0 °C < hysteresis < 5 °C

### p6393[0...n] Voltage model DC damping factor flux actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6732 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**ASM, PMSM, REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 | 5.0 | [ 0 ] 1.0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping factor for the DC quantity voltage model.  
In so doing, the change of flux with respect to time scaled with the damping factor
is applied to the EMF. The damping effect decreases with increasing value.

4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping factor for the DC quantity voltage model.  
In so doing, the second derivation of the flux setpoint change over time scaled with
the damping factor is applied to the EMF. The damping effect decreases with increasing
value.

### p6397[0...n] MSC phase shift second system

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the phase shift of the second system with respect to the first system for MSC
(motor-side converter)

**Value:**
  
0:
Shift by +30 °  
1:
Shift by -30 °  
2:
Shift by 0 °

**Note:**
  
For p6397 = 0 the following applies: The second systems lags.  
For p6397 = 1 the following applies: The second systems leads.

### p6501[0...62] External digital message, component assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8030 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 32767 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the component assignment of the external digital messages.  
The messages are issued on a component for component basis.  
p6501 = 0:  
The message is always issued.  
p6501 = A, A > 0:  
The message belongs to component A and is issued when component A is active.  
More information is provided in p7100 ... p7108.

A49000 (F) - BO: r6509.0

### p6507[0...7] External analog message component assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 32767 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the component assignment of the external analog messages.  
The messages are issued on a component for component basis.  
p6507 = 0:  
The message is always issued.  
p6507 = A, A > 0:  
The message belongs to component A and is issued when component A is active.  
More information is provided in p7100 ... p7108.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
A49090 (F) - BO: r6506.0  
[
1]:
A49091 (F) - BO: r6506.1  
[
2]:
A49092 (F) - BO: r6506.2  
[
3]:
F49093 (A) - BO: r6506.3  
[
4]:
F49094 (A) - BO: r6506.4  
[
5]:
F49095 (A) - BO: r6506.5  
[
6]:
F49096 (A) - BO: r6506.6  
[
7]:
F49097 (A) - BO: r6506.7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6503, p6504, p6505, r6506, p6508

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays for the status of the auxiliary fan.  
After the fault has been removed, p6517.x must be set = 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Cabinet 1 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 01 | Cabinet 1 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 02 | Cabinet 2 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 03 | Cabinet 2 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 04 | Cabinet 3 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 05 | Cabinet 3 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 06 | Cabinet 4 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 07 | Cabinet 4 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 08 | Cabinet 5 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 09 | Cabinet 5 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 10 | Cabinet 6 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 11 | Cabinet 6 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 12 | Cabinet 7 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 13 | Cabinet 7 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 14 | Cabinet 8 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 15 | Cabinet 8 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6515  
Refer to:
A49143, F49144

### r6543 HW-DESC Version CF

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the version of the hardware description data on the CompactFlash Card.  
The version is displayed in the form MAMIVA (major|minor|version, example: 010305).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6545, r6547  
Refer to:
F01275, A01276

### r6545[0...37] HW-DESC GUID CF

The individual characters of the GUID are displayed as ASCII code in the form {XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX}
in the indices.

F01275

An ASCII table (excerpt) can be found, for example, in the appendix to the List Manual.

### p6550[0...n] I2t motor monitoring activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the I2t motor monitoring function.  
0: I2t motor monitoring function deactivated.  
1: I2t motor monitoring function activated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0034, p0604, p0605, p0606, p6551, p6552, p6553, p6554, p6555  
Refer to:
A07972, F07973

**Notice:**
  
The output of the alarm or fault for the I2t motor monitoring must be activated (p6551,
p6552).  
The values for p6553, p6554 and p6555 must be appropriately adapted before activating
the monitoring function of the system, corresponding to the actual situation.

### p6551[0...n] I2t alarm output activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the alarm output for the I2t motor monitoring.  
When the selected alarm threshold is exceeded, the appropriate alarm is output.  
0: Alarm output deactivated.  
1: Alarm output activated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0604, p6550, p6553, p6554, p6555  
Refer to:
A07972

**Notice:**
  
The values for p6553, p6554 and p6555 must be appropriately adapted before activating
the monitoring function of the system, corresponding to the actual situation.

### p6552[0...n] I2t fault output activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the fault output for the I2t motor monitoring.  
When the selected fault threshold is exceeded or after the timer has expired for the
alarm threshold, the appropriate fault is output.  
0: Fault output deactivated  
1: Fault output activated

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0604, p0605, p0606, p6550, p6553, p6554, p6555  
Refer to:
F07973

**Notice:**
  
The values for p6553, p6554 and p6555 must be appropriately adapted before activating
the monitoring function of the system, corresponding to the actual situation.

### p6553[0...n] I2t motor monitoring thermal time constant

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 100 [s] | 10000 [s] | [ 0 ] 1000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the thermal motor time constant for the I2t motor monitoring function.  
Should be assigned 1/4 of the time that the motor requires at the rated voltage, rated
current and rated frequency as well as the ambient temperature to reach 98% of its
rated temperature.

### p6554[0...n] I2t motor monitoring cooling-down constant natural ventilation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 100 [s] | 10000 [s] | [ 0 ] 1000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the thermal time constant for the I2t motor monitoring when a naturally ventilated
motor cools down.  
Must be assigned 1/4 of the time that the naturally ventilated motor at standstill
requires to cool down from its rated temperature to the ambient temperature.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0335

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 | 1.000 | [ 0 ] 0.400 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Enters the point of a motor utilization characteristic for the I2t motor monitoring
of a naturally ventilated motor for f = 0.0 * rated motor frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0335

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

**Index:**
  
[
0]:

[
1]:

[
2]:

[
3]:

[
4]:

### r6575.0...29 CO/BO: PU control word/status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control word/status word of the power unit.  
For bit 23 = 1:  
The valve inhibit is active on at least one of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) assigned
to a drive object (DO).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Command door release on (PSA, DO 3) | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Circuit breaker enable (PSA, DO 1) | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Command precharging on (PSA, DO 2) | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Command circuit breaker undervoltage coil (PSA, DO 0) | Yes | No | - |
| 04 | Status precharging completed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Cell overvoltage fault active | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Status door opened (PSA, DI 1) | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Status PSA DI 3 | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Status leakage water detected (PSA, DI 2) | Yes | No | - |
| 23 | Status converter inhibited | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Status converter in a no-voltage condition | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Status AVT offset calibration active | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Status Vdc link monitoring active | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6901, p6902, p6903

### r6580[0...n] CO: PSA digital inputs/outputs status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the digital inputs/outputs of the Power
Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | Yes | No | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) | Yes | No | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) | Yes | No | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) | Yes | No | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Low | High | - |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command circuit breaker undervoltage coil | Yes | No | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) circuit breaker enable | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Yes | No | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) protective breaker anticondens. heating tripped | Yes | No | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output

### r6595[0...n] Cabinet 1 auxiliary fan 1 operating hours

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [h] | - [h] | [ ] - [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display of the operating hours of fan 1 in cabinet 1.  
Prerequisite:  
The operating hours were saved in a non-volatile fashion on the Power Stack Adapters
(PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p17750, p17751, p17752

**Note:**
  
This parameter is only relevant for firmware versions lower than V4.8.  
When upgrading from versions lower than V4.8, then the displayed value can be entered
in p17750, p17750 and p17752.  
The operating hours currently used in the software are saved in a non-volatile fashion
on the NVRAM of the Control Unit. They can be read and changed using parameters p17750,
p17751 and p17752.

| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the actual value of the DC link voltage.

### p6624[0...1] Submodule precharging supplementary setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [%]  [ 1 ] 20.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
For index [0]:  
Sets the supplementary setpoint for the total module voltage during step-up operation.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the voltage hysteresis of the DC link voltage during step-up operation.  
Both values are referred to half of the DC link setpoint (p3510).  
The cells are moderately overcharged as a result of the supplementary setpoint. A
transformer can be premagnetized with the additional cell energy.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Submodule precharging supplementary setpoint  
[
1]:
Submodule precharging Vdc hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The parameters are only active for p3400.2 = 1 (DC link capacitor available).

### r6630[0...1] DC link overvoltage tripping threshold crowbar thyristor PSA

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the trip threshold (PSA) for the crowbar thyristor due to a DC link overvoltage.  
r6630[0]:  
Displays the relative threshold referred to the maximum DC link voltage after having
taken into account the altitude derating.  
r6630[1]:  
Displays the absolute threshold that was written into the PSA after commissioning
was completed.

**Note:**
  
PSA: Power Stack Adapter

### p6650 Sequence control test mode activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 90 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate test mode for sequence control.  
For test mode [80]: the CU and DSM should be switched off and on again after the update.

**Value:**
  
0:
Test operation inactive  
7:
Test operation auxiliary fan  
10:
Test operation cooling  
12:
Test operation additional auxiliaries  
20:
Test operation motor side switch 1  
24:
Test operation output disconnector 1  
34:
Test operation main power supply  
50:
Test mode switch motor/line supply  
51:
Test mode switch motor/converter  
80:
Test operation DSM upgrade  
90:
Test operation circulating operation

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6651  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test mode.  
Test mode [80] is only performed after prior consultation with the development department
(Germany), and does not represent standard test operation while commissioning the
system.

**Caution:**
  
Bit r0899.3 (fault present) can be set with delay in the status word due to the message
generation.

**Note:**
  
The test is started via binector input p6651 = 0/1 signal.

### p6655 Acknowledgment external pulse time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the pulse time for the external acknowledgment.

**Note:**
  
The total duration of the acknowledgment procedure is extended by this time.  
The pulse time has been selected too short if a fault from the external peripherals
must be acknowledged twice.

### r6660.0...5 CO/BO: Switch 0 control signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

### p6670[0...5] BI: Switch simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the simulation mode for the switch.  
When the simulation mode is activated, the feedback signals for the switch are set
the same as the software commands. All monitoring functions are deactivated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the simulation mode.

**Note:**
  
BI: p6670[x] = 1 signal: Simulation mode activated  
BI: p6670[x] = 0 signal: Simulation mode deactivated

### p6671[0...5] BI: Switch on/off

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to close/open the switch.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Note:**
  
Switch 0 (motor-side circuit breaker) is only switched by the sequence control.  
Switch 1 (converter bypass switch) is only switched by the sequence control.  
Switch 2 (line bypass switch) is only switched by the sequence control.  
Switch 3 (motor side disconnector) is only switched by the sequence control.  
This means that binector input p6671[0-4] is inactive.  
BI: p6671[4] is used to control optional switches.

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 3600.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.150 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time for the feedback signal when closing the switch.  
After a CLOSE command, the feedback signal must be received within this time.

### p6675[0...5] Switch open monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.160 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the feedback signals when opening the switch.  
The feedback signal within the time in p6675 must indicate that the switch has reliably
opened (i.e. bounce can no longer be detected). The monitoring time in p6675 must
be set greater than the time in p6676 as it additionally takes into account the bounce
time of the feedback signal.  
The following applies: p6675 > p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6674, p6676  
Refer to:
F07311

**Danger:**
  
If this monitoring function responds, then it must be assumed that the switch had
not opened. In this case, the drive system is not disconnected from the line supply.

### p6676[0...5] Switch max. opening time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.100 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum permissible opening time of the switch.  
The feedback signal must indicate for the first time that the switch has opened within
the time in p6676. The feedback signal must be stable within the time in p6675 (i.e.
bounce can no longer be detected).  
The following applies: p6675 > p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07306

**Danger:**
  
The switch used must open within this time in order to protect the drive system.  
Switch with a longer opening time than specified in this parameter cannot be used.

4]:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |

| 01 | Feedback signal switch closed | No | Yes | - |

| 03 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 1 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 2 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Feedback signal no external trip | No | Yes | - |

| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal closed missing | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal open missing | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Too slow | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Auxiliary voltage 1 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Auxiliary voltage 2 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Consistency error | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Crowbar thyristor and feedback signal missing | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | External trip | No | Yes | - |

### p6695[0...n] BI: Undervoltage coil trip signal source 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets signal source 1 to trip the undervoltage coil of the circuit breaker.  
1 signal: command inactive  
0 signal: command active

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, p6696, p6697

**Note:**
  
The undervoltage coil of the circuit breaker is tripped via digital output 0 (DO 0,
X331.15) of the corresponding Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The AND logic operation of the following signals acts on digital output 0 of the PSA:  
- BI: p6695  
- BI: p6696  
- BI: p6697  
The trip is displayed using binector output r6575.3 = 0.

### p6700 Motor model smoothing

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 20 [%] | 500 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing of the flux orientation of the motor model for an induction motor
and a permanent-magnet synchronous motor.  
If p1402.12 = 1, using PLL, a smoothing time of 5 ms of the voltage model orientation
angle is applied for a separately excited synchronous motor for a setting value of
100 %.

### p6701[0...n] Motor voltage maximum steady state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6723, 6724 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50 [%] | 200 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum steady-state motor voltage for an induction motor and permanent-magnet
synchronous motor.  
The parameter is referred to the rated motor voltage. It defines the transition (corner)
point for the field weakening. If the motor voltage reaches the value specified in
p6701, then field weakening is initiated.

### p6702[0...1] Residual tachometer threshold standstill

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [%] | 500 [%] | [ 0 ] 2 [%]  [ 1 ] 3 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the hysteresis thresholds for the standstill (zero speed) detection of the residual
tachometer in encoderless operation.  
The value is referred to rated speed. When the pulses are inhibited, if the estimated
speed falls below the value saved in p6702[0], then a speed of 0 is displayed. A speed
is only displayed again when the upper hysteresis threshold (p6702[1]) is exceeded.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Residual tachometer standstill threshold lower  
[
1]:
Residual tachometer standstill threshold upper

**Caution:**
  
An unfavorable parameter value can result in the motor behaving in an uncontrollable
fashion.

### p6703 Absolute voltage threshold standstill

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 10.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.50 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold of the absolute voltage value for the standstill (zero speed) detection
for a separately excited synchronous motor in encoderless operation.  
Standstill is only detected if this threshold was not exceeded for a time corresponding
to p6704.

**Caution:**
  
An unfavorable parameter value can result in the motor behaving in an uncontrollable
fashion.

**Note:**
  
In the flying restart mode (p1200 = 1), standstill can only be detected for an excited
synchronous motor.

### p6704 Standstill detection delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [s] | 1000.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for the standstill (zero speed) detection for a separately excited
synchronous motor in encoderless operation.  
Standstill is only detected if, for the delay time, the threshold for the absolute
voltage value corresponding to p6703 is not exceeded.  
In the "flying restart" mode (p1200 = 1), standstill can only be detected for an excited
synchronous motor.

**Caution:**
  
An unfavorable parameter value can result in the motor behaving in an uncontrollable
fashion.

### p6705[0...n] Maximum excitation current

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10 [%] | 200 [%] | [ 0 ] 65 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum excitation current for the induction motor.  
The parameter is referred to the rated motor current.  
During magnetization, as a maximum, the value specified in p6705 is impressed (however,
as a minimum 110 % of the rated magnetizing current).  
The value can be reduced if, for a flying restart, a DC link overvoltage occurs.

### p6710[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The lower threshold defines the output for the first alarm.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07322

**Notice:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.

**Note:**
  
For p6710 = 0, the monitoring for the lower alarm threshold is switched out.

### p6711[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold upper

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The upper threshold defines the output for the second alarm.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07323

**Caution:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.  
When this alarm threshold is exceeded, the stress on the switch must be checked and
if required, the switch should be replaced.

**Note:**
  
For p6711 = 0, the monitoring for the upper alarm threshold is switched out.

### r6713[0...5] Switch switching operations, total

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the total number of switching operations of the switch.  
The total switching operations are determined as follows:  
r6713 = r6714 + r6715 * p6712  
r6713: Switching operations, total  
r6714: Switching operations in a no-current condition  
r6715: Switching operations under load  
p6712: Weighting factor for switching operations under load

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6710, p6711, p6712, p6714, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

### p6714[0...5] Switch switching operations in a no-current condition

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays and sets the number of switching operations of the switch under no-current
conditions.  
When setting the parameter, the value is accepted without confirmation.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6713, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

**Note:**
  
The total number of switching operations of the switch under no-current conditions
is saved in a non-volatile fashion on all of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) being
used.  
Inconsistency is not monitored and there is no automatic alignment of the switching
operations for several Power Stack Adapters. The values of the first Power Stack Adapter
are read and evaluated.

### p6716[0...1] BI: Bypass switch enable signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to enable the bypass switch.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable switch motor/converter   
[
1]:
Enable switch motor/line supply

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07919

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
BI: p6716[0] = 1 signal:  
The switch between the motor and converter is enabled and can be closed.  
For index [1]:  
BI: p6716[1] = 1 signal:  
The switch between the motor and the line supply is enabled and can be closed.

### p6717[0...3] BI: Motor side switch/disconnector enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the enable for the motor side switch or disconnector.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Disconnector PLC enable  
[
1]:
Motor side switch PLC enable  
[
2]:
Disconnector 2 PLC enable  
[
3]:
Motor side switch 2 PLC enable

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7000  
Refer to:
F07306, F07309

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
BI: p6717[0] = 1 signal  
The disconnector has been released and can be closed.  
BI: p6717[0] = 0 signal  
The disconnector is opened if current does not flow.  
For index [1]:  
BI: p6717[1] = 1 signal  
The motor side switch is enabled and can be closed.  
BI: p6717[1] = 0 signal  
The motor side switch is opened if OFF2 is active.  
For index [2]:  
BI: p6717[2] = 1 signal  
The second disconnector has been released and can be closed.  
BI: p6717[2] = 0 signal  
The second disconnector is opened if current does not flow.  
For index [3]:  
BI: p6717[3] = 1 signal  
The second motor side switch is enabled and can be closed.  
BI: p6717[3] = 0 signal  
The second motor side switch is opened if OFF2 is active.

### p6719 Motor side switch/disconn missing enable signal alarm delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 10.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.01 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for alarm A07919.  
The alarm is only output if the cause is present for a time longer than is set in
this parameter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07919

**Note:**
  
With p6719 = 0.00 s, Alarm A07919 is deactivated.

### r6727 CO: Offset commutation angle to pole wheel angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4715 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Offset angle between the electrical commutation angle (from the encoder) and the electrical
pole wheel angle.

**Note:**
  
If there is still no offset, cannot be determined – or if, with an encoder fault active,
value 3600 is displayed.  
The offset angle itself is either determined during the pole wheel position identification
(= rotor position sensing) with an incremental encoder, or from the absolute position
of the absolute encoder.  
The offset is also determined after an encoder fault has been resolved from the zero
track of the incremental encoder or from the absolute position.  
However, the precondition is that the setting is not equal to the factory setting
of parameter p6729.

### p6730[0...3] Motor model setpoint P mech pos smoothing time constant

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 500 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time constant of two PT1 smoothing functions for the setpoint of
the mechanical power for positive gradients of the mechanical power setpoint (power
increase).  
[0]: Field-weakening range of first PT1  
[1]: Field-weakening range of second PT1  
[2]: Base speed range of first PT1  
[3]: Base speed range of second PT1

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0082

**Note:**
  
The value formed in r0082[3] can be used to precontrol the infeed power. Further,
it is available in the precontrol network. Differences in the dynamic performance
of the infeed and motor can be equalized using these smoothing functions.  
The value 0 ms deactivates the relevant PT1 smoothing function.

it is available in the precontrol network. Differences in the dynamic performance
of the infeed and motor can be equalized using these smoothing functions.  
The value 0 ms deactivates the relevant PT1 smoothing function.

### p6734[0...7] Motor model precontrol power scaling field weakening

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [%] | 200 [%] | [ 0 ] 0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the evaluation factors for the various powers of the precontrol network in the
field weakening range.  
[0]: Setpoint, electrical power (motoring)  
[1]: Setpoint, mechanical power (motoring)  
[2]: Actual value, electrical power (motoring)  
[3]: Actual value, mechanical power (motoring)  
[4]: Setpoint, electrical power (regenerating)  
[5]: Setpoint, mechanical power (regenerating)  
[6]: Actual value, electrical power (regenerating)  
[7]: Actual value, mechanical power (regenerating)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0082, p6730, p6731, p6732, p6733, p6735

### p6737[0...n] Minimum motor speed when an output transformer is used

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [%] | 100000 [%] | [ 0 ] 50 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum motor speed when operating an induction motor with output transformer.  
The parameter is specified as a percentage of the rated slip.  
The starting torque can be influenced by changing the parameter.  
If the motor is locked or is very sluggish, then it is not permissible to select the
frequency too high as otherwise no flux will be able to be established.

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is only effective if parameters p0240 to p0242 are assigned.  
  
Refer to:
p0240, p0241, p0242, p0243, p0244

**Danger:**
  
The drive maintains this speed, even if a speed setpoint of zero is entered, as otherwise
it would lose its magnetization.  
The direction of rotation of the minimum speed depends on the direction limits as
follows:  
p1110 = 0 and p1111 = 0: direction of rotation positive  
p1110 = 0 and p1111 = 1: direction of rotation negative  
p1110 = 1 and p1111 = 0: direction of rotation positive  
p1110 = 1 and p1111 = 1: direction of rotation positive

**Note:**
  
For an OFF1, the minimum speed is fallen below and the drive returns to zero speed.

### p6738 CI: Motor model slip adaptation signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the motor model slip adaption.  
The slip frequency is multiplied by the content of the signal source. This allows
the temperature drift of the rotor resistance to be compensated.

| 0.00 [%] | 200.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the fine calibration for the actual value channel 10 (X80) for the Power Stack
Adapter (PSA).  
The measured actual value is multiplied by this factor.

### r6755[0...n] CO: PSA actual value channel 0 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the value of actual value channel 0 (X70) for the
Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The value is displayed corresponding to the set display mode (p6770).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6770

**Note:**
  
The actual value is scaled to the maximum mapping range of the shunt (-100.00 ...
100.00).  
Display value = 100.00 applies: The actual value sensing has failed.  
Display value = 9999.99 applies: The actual value sensing is not available.
