### p6770 PSA actual value channels display mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the required display mode for actual value channels for the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA).

**Value:**
  
0:
Not calibrated and with offset  
1:
Poss. calibrated and offset-free  
2:
Offset  
3:
AVT mapping range

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6755, r6756, r6757, r6758, r6759, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765

**Note:**
  
For p6770 = 1 the following applies:  
p6902, p6903 and p6904 define whether the offset calculation should be taken into
account.

### r6771[0...n] CO: FPGA register actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the content of the FPGA register cyclically transferred from the Power Stack
Adapter (PSA) to the Control Unit (CU).  
The required register is set using p6772.

### p6772[0...n] FPGA register display mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 73 | [ 0 ] 8 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the display mode for r6771 (FPGA register actual value).

**Value:**
  
0:
SET_VOLTAGE_1P setpoint voltage (calculated) arm 1P  
1:
SET_VOLTAGE_1N setpoint voltage (calculated) arm 1N  
2:
SET_VOLTAGE_2P setpoint voltage (calculated) arm 2P  
3:
SET_VOLTAGE_2N setpoint voltage (calculated) arm 2N  
4:
SET_VOLTAGE_3P setpoint voltage (calculated) arm 3P  
5:
SET_VOLTAGE_3N setpoint voltage (calculated) arm 3N  
6:
IDC_CTRL0-IDC control register  
7:
AVT_SIG_STAT status register actual value channels  
8:
TELEGRAM_ID sign of life character counter  
9:
DSM_STAT_LOCAL submodule status local  
10:
DSM_STAT_REMOTE submodule status remote  
11:
DSM_INFO0 submodule info word0  
12:
DSM_INFO1 submodule info word1  
13:

### r6773[0...n] CO: DSM number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the number of the currently evaluated double submodule
(DSM).  
The number changes cyclically alternating from 0 ... 35.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6774, r6775, r6776, r6777, r6778, r6779, p6784

**Note:**
  
This number is independent of the setting in p6784.

### r6778[0...n] CO: DSM register LOCAL

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the currently evaluated double submodule
(DSM).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Alarm "bit error" | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Alarm "poor signal quality" | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Fault "signal loss" | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Fault "corrupted code word" | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Fault "illegal code word" | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Info "all submodules signal signal lost" | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6773, p6784

### p6784 DSM index

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 72 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Selects a double submodule (PSM) to display the properties.  
For the setting 0, the DSM data are displayed cyclically alternating (from DSM00 to
DSM71).  
Parameter r6773 indicates to which DSM (0 ... 71) the displayed values refer.  
For the setting > 0, the data of the selected DSM are permanently displayed (independent
of r6773).  
p6784 = 1: displays the data of double submodule 0 (DSM00)  
...  
p6784 = 72: displays the data of double submodule 71 (DSM71)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6774, r6775, r6776, r6777, r6778, r6779

### p6787[0...3] Voltage measurement filter setting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 7 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the required filter coefficients for the voltage measurement.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Stage 1  
[
1]:
Stage 2  
[
2]:
Stage 3  
[
3]:
Stage 4

**Note:**
  
The voltage measured values are filtered using a recursive filter (4th order low-pass
filter).  
The parameter acts differently on the individual stages of the filter.  
p6780[0...3] = 0: Filter stage 0 ... 3 deactivated  
p6780[0...3] = 1 ... 7: Filter stage 0 ... 3 activated, the filter effect increases
from a minimum to a maximum

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 7 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

4.6

Sets the required filter coefficients for the current measurement.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Stage 1  
[
1]:
Stage 2  
[
2]:
Stage 3  
[
3]:
Stage 4

**Note:**
  
The current measured values are filtered using a recursive filter (4th order low-pass
filter).  
The parameter acts differently on the individual stages of the filter.  
p6780[0...3] = 0: Filter stage 0 ... 3 deactivated  
p6780[0...3] = 1 ... 7: Filter stage 0 ... 3 activated, the filter effect increases
from a minimum to a maximum

### p6790[0...3] Main fan differential pressure fault threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00000 | 1000.00000 | [ 0 ] 0.20000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault threshold for the differential pressure for the main fans.  
This is an absolute value in unit "pound per square inch" (psi).  
Fault F49124 is output if the differential pressure falls below this threshold.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
System 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
System 1 right cabinet  
[
2]:
System 2 left cabinet  
[
3]:
System 2 right cabinet

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6791  
Refer to:
F49124

Sets the differential pressure for the alarm threshold for the main fans.  
This is an absolute value in unit "pound per square inch" (psi).  
Alarm A49111 is output if the differential pressure falls below this threshold.

System 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
System 1 right cabinet  
[
2]:
System 2 left cabinet  
[
3]:
System 2 right cabinet

**Dependency:**

p6790

A49111

### p6794 Main fan monitoring time when switching on

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 60.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time for powering up/powering down the fans.  
If not all of the conditions are fulfilled within this monitoring time after powering
up/powering down, then an appropriate signal is output.

### p6795[0...1] Fan temperature threshold values

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 20.00 [°C] | 100.00 [°C] | [ 0 ] 30.00 [°C]  [ 1 ] 40.00 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the temperature threshold values for the air cooling.  
p6795[0]: When this threshold is exceeded, the sequence control of the fan transitions
into state "Fan setpoint 2", r6800[0...63]=5.  
p6795[1]: When this threshold is exceeded, the sequence control of the fan transitions
into state "Fan setpoint 3", r6800[0...63]=6.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6800

### p6797 Fan run-on time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5.00 [s] | 1800.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the run-on time when switching off the fans.

**Note:**
  
The minimum time of 5 seconds should not be fallen below as the control via Modbus
with 24 fans takes some time.

### p6798[0...16] BI: Fan feedback signals signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0...14 ] 1   [ 15 ] 0   [ 16 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the feedback signals from the fans.  
The signals are displayed in r6799[0...5].  
For bit 00,03: Feedback signal thermal protection switch T>5°C

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Thermal protection switch heat exchanger system 1  
[
1]:
Protective breaker power unit system 1  
[
2]:
Monitoring cabinet heating heat exchanger system 1  
[
3]:
Thermal protection switch heat exchanger system 2  
[
4]:
Protective breaker power unit system 2  
[
5]:
Monitoring cabinet heating heat exchanger system 2  
[
6...14]:
Reserved  
[
15]:
DC link precharged system 1  
[
16]:
DC link precharged system 2

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6799

### r6799.0...29 CO/BO: Fan status word/control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806, 9807 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the feedback signals and control commands of the fan control.  
For bits 00...05:  
Indicates the state of parameter p6798[0...5].  
For bits 06, 11  
At least one heat exchanger fan of the particular system is active (3 =< r6800[x]
=< 8).  
For bits 16...19:  
Alarm A49111 active.  
For bits 20...23:  
Fault F49124 active.  
For bits 26, 27  
At least one main fan of the particular system is active (3 =< r6800[x] =< 8).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Feedback signal thermal protection switch heat exchanger system1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 01 | Feedback signal protective breaker power unit system 1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 02 | Feedback signal cabinet heating heat exchanger system 1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 03 | Feedback signal thermal protection switch heat exchanger system1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 04 | Feedback signal protective breaker power unit system 2 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 05 | Feedback signal cabinet heating heat exchanger system 2 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 06 | Heat exchanger fan system 1 | Inactive | Active | 9807 |
| 07 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 1 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 08 | Command open air flap heat exchanger system 1 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 09 | Command supply voltage heat exchanger-inverter system 1 | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Heat exchanger fan system 2 | Inactive | Active | 9807 |
| 12 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 2 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 13 | Command open air flap heat exchanger system 2 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 14 | Command supply voltage heat exchanger-inverter system 2 | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Alarm differential pressure system 1 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 17 | Alarm differential pressure system 1 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 18 | Alarm differential pressure system 2 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 19 | Alarm differential pressure system 2 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 20 | Fault differential pressure system 1 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 21 | Fault differential pressure system 1 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 22 | Fault differential pressure system 2 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 23 | Fault differential pressure system 2 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 24 | DSM overtemperature system 1 | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 25 | DSM overtemperature system 2 | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 26 | Cabinet fan system 1 | Inactive | Active | 9806 |
| 27 | Cabinet fan system 2 | Inactive | Active | 9806 |
| 28 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 1 requested | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 29 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 2 requested | No | Yes | 9806 |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6798

### r6800[0...63] CO: Fan sequence control status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 9 | [ ] - |

5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the fans.

**Value:**

Initialization  
1:
Fan off  
2:
Switch on fan  
3:
Fan precharging  
4:
Fan setpoint 1  
5:
Fan setpoint 2  
6:
Fan setpoint 3  
7:
Fan setpoint 4  
8:
Fan run-on time  
9:
Fan fault

8]:

[
9]:

[
10]:

[
11]:
Heat exchanger fan 12 system 1 left cabinet  
[
12...15]:
Reserved  
[
16]:
Heat exchanger fan 14 system 1 right cabinet  
[
17]:
Main fan 15 system 1 right cabinet  
[
18]:
Heat exchanger fan 16 system 1 right cabinet  
[
19]:
Main fan 17 system 1 right cabinet  
[
20]:
Heat exchanger fan 18 system 1 right cabinet  
[
21]:
Main fan 19 system 1 right cabinet  
[
22]:
Heat exchanger fan 20 system 1 right cabinet  
[
23]:

[
24]:
Heat exchanger fan 22 system 1 right cabinet  
[
25]:
Main fan 23 system 1 right cabinet  
[
26]:
Heat exchanger fan 24 system 1 right cabinet  
[
27]:
Reserved (main fan 25 system 1 right cabinet)  
[
28...31]:
Reserved  
[
32]:
Main fan 1 system 2 left cabinet  
[
33]:
Heat exchanger fan 2 system 2 left cabinet  
[
34]:
Main fan 3 system 2 left cabinet  
[
35]:
Heat exchanger fan 4 system 2 left cabinet  
[
36]:
Main fan 5 system 2 left cabinet  
[
37]:
Heat exchanger fan 6 system 2 left cabinet  
[
38]:

[
39]:

[
40]:

[
41]:

[
42]:

[
44...47]:
Reserved  
[

Heat exchanger fan 14 system 2 right cabinet  
[
49]:
Main fan 15 system 2 right cabinet  
[
50]:

51]:

52]:

53]:

54]:

[
55]:

[
56]:
Heat exchanger fan 22 system 2 right cabinet  
[
57]:
Main fan 23 system 2 right cabinet  
[
58]:
Heat exchanger fan 24 system 2 right cabinet  
[
59]:

[
60...63]:
Reserved

**Note:**
  
Every fan is controlled by its own converter via Modbus.

### r6801[0...7] CO: Fan actual values

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p0514 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the following values for the main fans:  
- Differential pressure actual values in unit "pound per square inch" (psi).  
- Temperature actual values in degrees Celsius.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure system 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
Differential pressure system 1 right cabinet  
[
2]:
Differential pressure system 2 left cabinet  
[
3]:
Differential pressure system 2 right cabinet  
[
4]:
Temperature system 1 left cabinet  
[
5]:
Temperature system 1 right cabinet  
[
6]:
Temperature system 2 left cabinet  
[
7]:
Temperature system 2 right cabinet

**Note:**
  
The actual values are scaled as follows:  
- r6801[0..3] = p6803[1, 3, 5, 7] x p6804[1, 3, 5, 7]  
- r6801[4..7] = p6803[0, 2, 4, 6] x p6804[0, 2 ,4 ,6]

### p6804[0...7] Main fan scaling factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -340.28235E36 | 340.28235E36 | [ 0 ] 1.000000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the scaling factor for the actual temperature and differential pressure values
for the main fans.  
The actual values are interconnected via connector input p6803[0...7].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature system 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
Differential pressure system 1 left cabinet  
[
2]:
Temperature system 1 right cabinet  
[
3]:
Differential pressure system 1 right cabinet  
[
4]:
Temperature system 2 left cabinet  
[
5]:
Differential pressure system 2 left cabinet  
[
6]:
Temperature system 2 right cabinet  
[
7]:
Differential pressure system 2 right cabinet

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6803

**Note:**
  
The actual values are scaled as follows:  
- r6801[0..3] = p6803[1, 3, 5, 7] x p6804[1, 3, 5, 7]  
- r6801[4..7] = p6803[0, 2, 4, 6] x p6804[0, 2 ,4 ,6]  
For example, the conversion factor from unit "inch water column" to "pound per square
inch" (psi) 0.0360912.

### p6814 Braking chopper total module voltage threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7622 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 30.00 [%] | 135.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 120.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the overvoltage threshold at the double submodules.  
The value is referred to the nominal total module voltage (hardware description data).  
If the actual value of the total module voltage r0070[0] exceeds this threshold, then
a negative current setpoint is generated for the braking chopper and is made available
at connector output r6818 for interconnection; for example, to switch on the braking
chopper.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6817, r6818

### p6850[0...n] PSA analog output 0 AND logic operation value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the value to be ANDed with the signal source in connector input p6840 for analog
output 0 (X332.15/X332.16) of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Signal sources with data type Unsigned16/Unsigned32 are ANDed with this selected value.  
With the factory setting of p6860 and p6870 the result is displayed as follows at
the analog output:  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 1 --> analog output = 10 V  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 0 --> analog output = 5 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

output 7 (X332.1/X332.2) of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Signal sources with data type Unsigned16/Unsigned32 are ANDed with this selected value.  
With the factory setting of p6867 and p6877 the result is displayed as follows at
the analog output:  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 1 --> analog output = 10 V  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 0 --> analog output = 5 V

### r6888[0...3] CO: PSA analog outputs calibration signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Calibration signals for the analog outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
For the factory settings of p6860 to p6867 and p6870 to p6877, by appropriately interconnecting
connector input CI: p6840 to p6847 with r6888[0...3], the required calibration signals
are effective at the analog output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Constant value -1.0 --> 0 V  
[
1]:
Constant value 0.0 --> 5 V  
[
2]:
Constant value +1.0 --> 10 V  
[
3]:
Sawtooth with 1 Hz from -1.0 ... +1.0 --> 0 ... 10 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

**Note:**
  
When setting the scaling factor (p6842) and offset (p6843) to the factory setting,
the following apply:  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[0] --> analog output = 0 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[1] --> analog output = 5 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[2] --> analog output = 10 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[3] --> analog output = sawtooth 0 ...
10 V with 1 Hz

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 06890 - 07587

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORM2C 06890 - 07587

### p6890[0...n] Setpoint transmitter starting value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6301 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting of the starting value at zero frequency for the setpoint transmitter.  
U/f control (p1300 = 0):  
The value refers to the rated motor voltage (p0304).  
I/f control (p1300 = 18):  
The value refers to the rated motor current (p0305).

### p6891[0...n] Setpoint transmitter, maximum amplitude

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum possible amplitude of the setpoint transmitter output.  
U/f control (p1300 = 0):  
The voltage amplitude of the setpoint transmitter output is limited to this value.
The value refers to the rated motor voltage (p0304).  
I/f control (p1300 = 18):  
The current amplitude of the setpoint transmitter output is limited to this value.
The value refers to the rated motor current (p0305).

### p6901 DC link voltage actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for DC link voltage actual value sensing.  
When the mode is enabled (p6901 = 0) for an operating inhibit, the offset is determined.  
For the inhibited mode (p6901 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.  
If the mode is saved (p6901 = 2), the offset determined in enabled mode is saved and
is subsequently taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of
the operating state (unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
When the mode is enabled (p6902 = 0), the offset is automatically determined when
the converter is in a no-current state. If it is not possible to detect that there
is no current flowing (e.g. a slowly rotating motor without encoder), then the user
must manually perform the calibration (p6902 = 0 in the no-current state, and then
p6902 = 2 and then subsequently save the parameter).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, r6755, r6756, r6757, r6758, r6759, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765,
r6766  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

### p6902 Current actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for the current actual value sensing.  
If the mode is enabled (p6902 = 0), for an operating inhibit, the offset is determined
and when operation is enabled, the determined offset is taken into account for the
actual value calculation.  
For the inhibited mode (p6902 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.  
If the mode is saved (p6902 = 2), the offset determined in enabled mode is saved and
is subsequently taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of
the operating state (unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
When the mode is enabled (p6902 = 0), the offset is automatically determined when
the converter is in a no-current state. If it is not possible to detect that there
is no current flowing (e.g. a slowly rotating motor without encoder), then the user
must manually perform the calibration (p6902 = 0 in the no-current state, and then
p6902 = 2 and then subsequently save the parameter).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765, r6766, r6767, r6768, r6769, p6903,
p6950  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

| --- | --- | --- |

in the hardware.

| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_2 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [K] | - [K] | [ ] - [K] |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

model.  
The value is referred to the actual shutdown threshold of the power semiconductors

### p6932[0...n] CI: Thermal converter model temperature input signal source 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the temperature input of the thermal model.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6933, r6934

**Notice:**
  
Possible failure of the temperature sensor can result in the semiconductor temperature
being incorrectly calculated. The following response can be set, depending on the
parameterization of p6932/p6933:  
1) p6932 and p6933 are connected with different temperature signal sources. The maximum
from two sources is processed here.  
2) p6932 and p6933 are connected with the same temperature signal source. In the case
that the source develops a fault, internally, the maximum cooling water temperature
(40°C) is assumed. The converter would possibly shut down earlier than necessary.  
3) Either p6932 or p6933 is permanently interconnected to 0, and the other parameters
are connected with the temperature signal source. In the case of a fault, internally,
a cooling water temperature of 0°C is assumed so that the converter would be switched
off too late. This could damage the converter. In this specific case, it is recommended
that p6932 and p6933 are connected with the same temperature signal source.

**Note:**
  
The value is displayed via connector output r6934.

from two sources is processed here.

| 20 | PSA AT 2 configuration | Fixed | Freely used | - |

### p6944[0...n] PSA analog inputs type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9984 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0011 0011 0011 0011 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the type of analog inputs on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
In addition, the associated switches S5 and S6 must be appropriately set.  
AI 0: S5.0 = V --> voltage input, S5.0 = I --> current input  
AI 1: S5.1 = V --> voltage input, S5.1 = I --> current input  
AI 2: S6.0 = V --> voltage input, S6.0 = I --> current input  
AI 3: S6.1 = V --> voltage input, S6.1 = I --> current input  
The scaling (final value) can be set using parameters p17460, p17463, p17466, p17469.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA AI 0 , type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 01 | PSA AI 0, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 02 | PSA AI 0, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | PSA AI 1, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 05 | PSA AI 1, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 06 | PSA AI 1, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | PSA AI 2, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 09 | PSA AI 2, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 10 | PSA AI 2, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | PSA AI 3, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 13 | PSA AI 3, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 14 | PSA AI 3, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6946, r6947, r6948, r6949, p17460, r17462, p17463, r17465, p17466, r17468, p17469,
r17471  
Refer to:
A30125

**Notice:**
  
For operation as a voltage input/current input, switch S5.0, S5.1, S6.0 or S6.1 must
be appropriately set.

**Note:**
  
The settings of the individual inputs can only be changed if these are not preset
by the hardware description (DESC0000).

### p6945[0...n] PSA temperature sensor type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the sensor type for Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Temperature input (Pt100) is enabled. The input is monitored for wire breakage and
short-circuit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA AT 0 , type | Open | PT100 | - |
| 04 | PSA AT 1, type | Open | PT100 | - |
| 08 | PSA AT 2, type | Open | PT100 | - |
| 12 | PSA AT 3, type | Open | PT100 | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6951, r6952, r6953, r6954

**Note:**
  
The settings of the individual inputs can only be changed if these are not preset
by the hardware description (DESC0000).

### p6950[0...2] Actual value monitoring threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 110.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the actual value monitoring function.  
The value refers to the maximum resolution range of the particular actual value channel.  
For p6770 = 3, this value is displayed for the actual value channel involved in the
corresponding parameter (r6760 and following).  
The total threshold value is displayed there for p6770 = 4.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DC link voltage  
[
1]:
Output current  
[
2]:
Output voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30106

### r6951[0...n] CO: PSA temperature input 0 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°C] | - [°C] | [ ] - [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the smoothed value at temperature input 0 (TEMP 0)
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6955, p6959  
Refer to:
A30018, A30019

**Note:**
  
The input signal can be smoothed for all PSA using p6959[4].  
For 4-conductor temperature sensors, the following applies  
I_IN 0: X333.13  
TEMP 0-: X333.14  
TEMP 0+: X333.15  
I_OUT 0: X333.16  
For 2-conductor temperature sensors, the following external jumpers are required:  
X333.13 - X333.14  
X333.15 - X333.16  
For a display value = -200 °C, the following applies:  
The input is not enabled via parameter p6945 or via the hardware description data.  
For a display value = -199 °C, the following applies:  
An implausible measured value is available at the input.

### p6955[0...n] PSA temperature input 0 offset

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -100.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the temperature offset for the PT100 measured value at PSA temperature input
0 (PSA TEMP 0).  
If there is a difference between the temperature displayed in r6951 and the actual
temperature, then this offset can be entered in this parameter. thereby compensating
for the difference.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, p6959

### p6959[0...7] PSA analog inputs smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9984, 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [ms] | 1000.0 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time constant of the 1st order low-pass filter for the analog inputs
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
This setting is the same for all PDS.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Analog input 0  
[
1]:
Analog input 1  
[
2]:
Analog input 2  
[
3]:
Analog input 3  
[
4]:
Temperature input 0  
[
5]:
Temperature input 1  
[
6]:
Temperature input 2  
[
7]:
Temperature input 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, r6952, r6954, p6955

**Note:**
  
AI: Analog Input  
TEMP: temperature sensor input PT100

### r6992.0...15 CO/BO: Recorder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the recorder.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 01 | External activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 02 | Internal trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 03 | External trigger 1.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 04 | External trigger 1.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 05 | External trigger 1.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 06 | External trigger 1.4 | Not set | Set | - |
| 07 | External trigger 2.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 08 | External trigger 2.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 09 | External trigger 2.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 10 | Hardware trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 11 | Data buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Post trigger time running | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Data being stored | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Data buffer full | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Trigger group signal | Not set | Set | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993, p6994, r6997, p6998, p6999  
Refer to:
A49998

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0001 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the bit mask for trigger signal 2 (p6994) of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6994

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

### p6998[0...4] BI: Recorder trigger 1 signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources to activate and trigger the recorder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External activation  
[
1]:
External trigger 1.1  
[
2]:
External trigger 1.2  
[
3]:
External trigger 1.3  
[
4]:
External trigger 1.4

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
The external recorder activation extends the internal activation. If the recorder
has already been activated internally, then it can be externally triggered via p6998[1..4]
or p6994[0..2], without requiring that p6998[0] = 1.

### p6999[0...8] Recorder parameterization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2000 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 2000   [ 2 ] 1900   [ 3 ] 0   [ 4 ] 0   [ 5 ] 100   [ 6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to parameterize the recorder.  
The recorder supplies up to 256 internal variables (depending on the parameterization).
The maximum recording time is 2000 ms. The variables are acquired in an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time – and a pretrigger can be set. The values
can then be acyclically written to the memory card.  
The development and system test departments have the software necessary to decode
the content.  
Normally, this recorder is always enabled. However, if problems occur for topologies
with a low remaining CPU time, then it can be deactivated using this parameter (this
saves approx. 5 ... 20 µs CPU time).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable  
[
1]:
Recording time  
[
2]:
Pretrigger time internal trigger  
[
3]:
Output message  
[
4]:
Recording factor  
[
5]:
Pretrigger time external trigger  
[
6]:
Hardware trace in synchronism  
[
7]:
Hardware trigger active  
[
8]:
Recording sampling time factor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49998

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
Enables or disables the function.  
p6999[0] = 0  
Inhibits the function.  
p6999[0] = 1  
Enables the function. A POWER ON is required after the enable.  
p6999[0] = 20  
Sets the factory setting for p6999, p6996, p6993, p6994, p6998.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the recording time, [ms]. The value is discretized with the current controller
sampling time.  
For index [2]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the internal trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [3]:  
Enables or inhibits the output of message A49998 when a trigger event is initiated.  
For index [4]:  
p6999[4] = n, n = 0 ... 4  
Recording with the factor, which extends the record time p6999[1] and the pre-trigger
time p6999[2] 2^n times, and reduces the number of signals 2^n times.  
Example:  
p6999[1] = 2000, p6999[2] = 1000, p6999[4] = 4  
--> recording time: 2000 ms * 2^4 = 32 s, pre-trigger time:1000 ms * 2^4 = 16 s, number
of signals 128/16 = 8  
When setting p6999[4] to 0, the results are as follows:  
--> recording time: 2000 ms, pre-trigger time: 1000 ms, number of signals: 128  
The maximum number of signals depends on the drive object.  
For index [5]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the external trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [6]:  
Activating/deactivating wait time for a hardware trace to be completely uploaded.  
p6999[6] = 1  
After a trigger event, the system waits for the hardware trace upload to be completed.
A new trigger is not possible until this upload has been completed.  
p6999[6] = 0  
After a trigger event, the system does NOT wait for the hardware trace upload to be
completed. Recorder trace can be immediately re-triggered.  
For index [7]:  
Deactivates/activates the hardware trigger. When p6999[7] = 0, the hardware trigger
is deactivated, and a hardware fault means that the recorder is no longer triggered.  
For index [8]:  
Sets the factor for the recording sampling time.  
Example:  
p6999[8] = 2 --> sampling time to record/trace signals is 2 x current controller sampling
time  
After a power on, this parameter has the same value as before the change.

### r7100[0...99] Par_circuit ring buffer fault/alarm code

| --- | --- | --- |

Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred from power units connected in

### r7222[0...n] CO: Par_circuit absolute current actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |

### p7510 Power unit identification type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 36 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the identification type for the power unit.

**Value:**
  
0:
Identification off  
10:
Identification C submodule and L arm reactor  
11:
Identification L DC link  
12:
Step response VCQM control loop  
13:
Step response VAGDC control loop DC  
14:
Step response V0G control loop  
15:
Step response V0M control loop  
16:
Step response modulation depth reserve control loop  
30:
Step response VC0M difference control loop  
31:
Step response VCM difference control loop  
32:
Step response VC0G difference control loop  
33:
Step response VCG difference control loop  
34:
Step response IG difference control loop  
35:
NA reserve 1  
36:
NA reserve 2

### p7528[0...n] DC link current limit positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7603 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.01 [A] | 100000.00 [A] | [ 0 ] 100000.00 [A] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive limit for the DC link current.

**Note:**
  
For a positive DC link current, a motor application (VECTORM2C) is motoring.  
In this case, a grid application (A_INFM2C) injects power into the grid.  
The parameter is preassigned when commissioning for the first time.

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the negative limit for the DC link current.

**Note:**
  
For a positive DC link current, a motor application (VECTORM2C) is motoring.  
In this case, a grid application (A_INFM2C) injects power into the grid.  
The parameter is preassigned when commissioning for the first time.

### p7530[0...n] DC link voltage setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50 [%] | 116 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint for the DC link voltage when the infeed is active in operating mode
p1300 = 21.  
The parameter refers to the rated DC link voltage of the converter.

**Dependency:**
  
The total setpoint of the DC link voltage comprises the fixed value (p7530) and the
supplementary value (p7531). The sum has an upper limit of 116 % of the rated DC link
voltage. The sum has a lower limit defined by p7532.  
  
Refer to:
p7531, p7532

### p7531 CI: DC link voltage supplementary setpoint signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the supplementary setpoint of the DC link voltage when
the infeed is active in operating mode p1300 = 21.  
The parameter refers to the rated DC link voltage of the converter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7530, p7532

### p7532[0...n] Minimum DC link voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50 [%] | 90 [%] | [ 0 ] 80 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower limit for the DC link voltage.  
A lower limit is applied to the DC link voltage if the rectified value of the line
voltage falls below the value set in p7532. Power can no longer be drawn from the
DC link. When the Vdc_min controller (kinetic buffering) is enabled, the closed-loop
controller starts to support the module voltages.  
The parameter refers to the rated DC link voltage of the converter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7530, p7531

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 [%] | 100 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the scaling of the torque ramp after intervention of the Vdc_min controller (kinetic
buffering).  
After a power failure and intervention of the Vdc_min controller, when the power supply
returns, the torque is ramped up along a 50 ms ramp. The ramp gradient can be scaled
with p7535, and can therefore be reduced. This can be used to avoid resonance points
being excited in the drive train.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7536

### p7536 Vdc_min controller ramp-up time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 1000.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the ramp-up time of the ramp-function generator after the Vdc_min controller
has intervened (kinetic buffering).  
After a power failure and intervention of the Vdc_min controller, the ramp-function
generator is set to the speed actual value. When the power supply returns, the ramp-function
generator ramps up with the time set in p7536.  
When p7536 = 0, the standard up-ramp of the ramp-function generator (p1120) is used.

### p7541[0...n] Switchover frequency clocked modulation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5.00 [Hz] | 300.00 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 34.00 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower switchover frequency, where the circulating currents are modulated,
clocked.  
The ripple on the capacitor currents can be optimized using this frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
The upper switchover frequency is specified in p7542. It must be greater than p7541,
so that a hysteresis is obtained.  
  
Refer to:
p7542

### p7542[0...n] Frequency hysteresis between pulsed/optimized modulation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [Hz] | 300.00 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 2.00 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the frequency hysteresis between pulsed and optimized modulation of the circulating
currents.  
The ripple on the capacitor currents can be optimized using this frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
The lower switchover frequency is specified in p7541.  
The upper limit is obtained from p7541+p7542.  
  
Refer to:
p7541

### p7545[0...n] Maximum conductor-ground voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [V] | 100000.00 [V] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum conductor-ground voltage at the converter terminals.  
This value is automatically preset to 71 % of the rated line voltage or the rated
motor voltage using p3900 or p0340 when commissioning has been completed.  
The value can be increased for motors with adequate insulation; as a consequence,
a higher output current is obtained at low frequencies.

### r7760 Write protection/know-how protection status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status for the write protection and know-how protection.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Write protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Know-how protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Know-how protection temporarily withdrawn | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Know-how protection cannot be deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Extended copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Basic copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Trace and measuring functions for diagnostic purposes active | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Reserved, Siemens-internal | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7761, p7765

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
For bit 00:  
Write protection can be activated/deactivated via p7761 on the Control Unit.  
For bit 01:  
The know-how protection can be activated by entering a password (p7766 ... p7768).  
For bit 02:  
If it has already been activated, know-how protection can be temporarily deactivated
by entering the valid password in p7766. In this case, bit 1 = 0 and bit 2 = 1 offset.  
For bit 03:  
Know-how protection cannot be deactivated, as p7766 is not entered in the OEM exception
list (only the factory setting is possible). This bit is only set if know-how protection
is active (bit 1 = 1) and p7766 has not been entered in the OEM exception list.  
For bit 04:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards/Control
Units. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and in p7765.0 is set
= 1.  
For bit 05:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards.
This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.1 is set = 1 and p7765.0
is set = 0.  
For bit 06:  
When know-how protection is activated, the drive data can be traced using the device
trace function. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.2
is set = 1.  
For bit 12:  
Together with p7755, the bit is used to monitor write protection.  
Bit = 1, if p7755 is not equal to 0 and write protection is active (r7760.0 = 1).  
Bit = 0, if write protection was deactivated. p7755 is set to 0, and when write protection
is activated again, bit 12 remains at 0.

### p7770 NVRAM action

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the action to be executed for NVRAM data.  
At the end of the action the value is automatically set to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Load NVRAM data to parameters  
2:
Load parameters to NVRAM  
3:
Reset

**Notice:**
  
After action p7770 = 1 no more pulses may be enabled.  
After action p7770 = 2, it is essential that parameters are backed up (p0977 = 1)
and that a warm restart is then performed (p0009 = 30, p0976 = 2, 3). This will apply
the values written.

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
This action loads the NVRAM data to the parameters.  
For value = 2:  
This action loads the parameters to the NVRAM.  
If value = 3:  
This action sets parameters p7771 ... p7774 to the factory setting.  
It is recommended to avoid placing unnecessary load on the subsequent upload/download
operation.

### r7832[0...23] Telegram diagnostics numerical format

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the original numerical format of the signals contained in the telegram.  
The associated signal number is represented in the appropriate index of r7831.

**Value:**
  
-1:
Unknown  
0:
Boolean  
1:
Signed 1 byte  
2:
Signed 2 byte  
3:
Signed 4 byte  
4:
Signed 8 byte  
5:
Unsigned 1 byte  
6:
Unsigned 2 byte  
7:
Unsigned 4 byte  
8:
Unsigned 8 byte  
9:
Float 4 byte  
10:
Double 8 byte  
11:
mm dd yy HH MM SS MS DOW  
12:
ASCII string  
13:
SINUMERIK frame type  
14:
SINUMERIK axis type

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7831

### p7857 Sub-boot mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode for the sub-boot.

**Value:**
  
0:
Sub-boot manual  
1:
Sub-boot automatic

**Note:**
  
For p7857 = 0 (manual sub-boot) the following applies:  
The parameter should be set to 1 to start the sub-boot.

### r7871[0...15] Configuration changes drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the configuration changes on the drive object.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Sum of the following indices  
[
1]:
p0010, p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173  
[
2]:
Drive object name (p0199)  
[
3]:
Structure-relevant parameters (e.g. p0180)  
[
4]:
BICO interconnections  
[
5]:
Activate/deactivate drive object  
[
6]:
Data backup required  
[
7]:
Activate/deactivate component  
[
8]:
Reference or changeover parameters (e.g. p2000)  
[
9]:
Parameter count through Drive Control Chart (DCC)  
[
10]:
p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173  
[
11]:
p0530 or p0531  
[
12]:
Write protection and know-how protection status  
[
13]:
Reserved  
[
14]:
Reserved  
[
15]:
SERVO or VECTOR (e.g. p0300)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7868, r7870

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
When changing one of the following indices, then the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [1]:  
Drive object commissioning: When changing p0010, p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173,
the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [2]:  
Drive object name. When changing p0199, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [3]:  
Drive object structure. When changing a parameter that is relevant for the structure
(e.g. number of data sets), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [4]:  
Drive object BICO interconnections. When changing r3977, the value in this index is
incremented.  
For index [5]:  
Drive object activity: When changing p0105, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [6]:  
Drive object, data save.  
0: There are no parameter changes to save.  
1: There are parameter changes to save.  
For index [7]:  
Drive object component activity: When changing either p0125 or p0145, the value in
this index is incremented.  
For index [8]:  
Drive object changeover of units. When changing reference or changeover parameters
(e.g. p2000, p0304), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [9]:  
Drive object parameter count. When changing the number of parameters by loading Drive
Control Chart (DCC), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [10]:  
Drive object configuration. When changing p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173, the
value in this index is incremented.  
For index [11]:  
Drive object bearing. When changing p0530 or p0531, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [12]:  
Drive object configuration. When activating/deactivating write protection or know-how
protection, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [15]:  
SERVO/VECTOR configuration. When changing p0300, p0301 or p0400, the value in this
index is incremented.

### r7872[0...3] Drive object status changes

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status changes on the drive object.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Sum of the following indices  
[
1]:
Faults (r0944)  
[
2]:
Alarms (r2121)  
[
3]:
Safety messages (r9744)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7869

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
When changing one of the following indices, then the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [1]:  
Drive object faults. When changing r0944, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [2]:  
Drive object alarms. When changing r2121, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [3]:  
Drive object safety messages. When changing r9744, the value in this index is incremented.

### p8612[0...1] CAN drive object server SDO

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN (CAN), CU_MV_DP (CAN), VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0581 hex | 8000 067F hex | [ 0 ] 8000 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the identifier (client/server and server/client) of the additional SDO server.  
Using these SDO servers, it is possible to access manufacturer-specific CANopen objects
of the supported drive objects.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
COB-ID drive object from the client to the server  
[
1]:
COB-ID drive object from the server to the client

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8610

**Note:**
  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Regarding the drive object Control Unit:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1201 hex  
Regarding the drive object with closed-loop control functions:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1202 hex + 1 * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7)

### p8641 CAN Abort Connection Option Code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 3 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault response for a CAN communication error with output of fault F08700.  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 6007 hex.

**Value:**
  
0:
No response  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2  
3:
OFF3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2100, p2101  
Refer to:
F08700

**Note:**
  
When the factory setting is changed, the fault response of fault F08700 is written
to p2100 and p2101. In this particular case it is not possible to change the message
type using p2118 and p2119.

### p8700[0...1] CAN Receive PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9204, 9206 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | 8000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] 8000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Receive Process Data Object 1 (RPDO
1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1400 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1, FE and FF can be set.  
PDO: Process Data Object
